programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: The mother of a model threatens to kill anyone who proposes to her daughter, and becomes the prime suspect when a private investigator is murdered. Lesley-Anne Down guest stars

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Jesse seeks advice from Dr Sloan when he suspects an improper procedure was the cause of a patient's death, and the pair become embroiled in a $2million life insurance fraud, in which a renowned surgeon is the prime suspect

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: An undercover police officer risks life and limb to infiltrate a terrorist group intent on causing mayhem and destruction to further its agenda. Drama, guest starring Tracey Gold, with Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: While tracking a device that can prevent nuclear meltdown through the sewers, Walter learns the nuances of listening and not offering advice to Paige.

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: For Sylvester's birthday, Team Scorpion heads to a Renaissance festival, but the revelry is cut short when a police evidence locker nearby is robbed

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A deep-sea diver is murdered on the job, and his co-workers all become suspects. However, when they return to the surface, they must remain in a high-tech decompression chamber for four days. With each of their suspects confined within a tank, the NCIS team must complete an unorthodox investigation from the outside. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Sarah Porter, the Secretary of the Navy, learns her daughter has been kidnapped, and the NCIS crew teams up with the FBI to track the case, determine a motive and bring the abducted girl home. Meanwhile, McGee's childhood friend, NCIS Special Agent Valerie Page, arrives in town to assist with advanced tactics training. Naval crime drama, guest starring Leslie Hope and Christina Chang

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team looks into a raid on a naval base, and Dr Cyril Taft - the surgeon who saved Gibbs' life - experiences the thrill of assisting on an investigation. Assigned to treat Gibbs' suspect, Taft manages to uncover evidence that may prove crucial to solving the case. Meanwhile, DiNozzo pays his old flame Jeanne Benoit a visit as he chases a lead involving doctors in Sudan. Guest starring John Cryer and Scottie Thompson

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A day spent working on a cut-and-dried case leaves the team free to spend some time away from work, and while McGee and Delilah argue over the importance of keeping shop-talk to a minimum, DiNozzo ends up boring his dinner date with crime-solving tales. However, the agents' personal plans are all interrupted as, one-by-one, they realise errors have been made in closing their latest investigation. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An American couple are attacked in Iraq, in the very same spot a group of US Special Ops snipers were ambushed six months earlier. Determined to get to the bottom of the case, Gibbs is forced to speak to Aaron Davis, the only survivor of the first assault, who is now struggling through PTSD at the Walter Reed Medical Centre. The 300th episode of the military crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when two pharmaceutical executives are murdered, uncovering a criminal who changes his identity for each offence he commits, and has evaded capture across several countries. To make matters worse, the agents are made to feel the pressure by the watchful eye of Granger. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and Miguel Ferrer

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part one of two. Kensi is taken into custody as chief suspect in the murder of Marine Sergeant Chris Black, but fancies her chances of catching the real killer herself. Meanwhile, Callen and the rest of the team work to prove their colleague's innocence. Crime drama, starring Daniela Ruah and Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part two of two. Kensi goes in search of her father's killer, before bumping into her mother, who she has not seen for more than 15 years. Meanwhile, the discovery that a member of Kensi's father's unit faked his own death prompts Granger to back down from his suspicions of his colleague. Crime drama, starring Daniela Ruah and Miguel Ferrer

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team is forced to establish whether the gunning down of a man outside the Vietnamese Consulate is an isolated incident or a terrorist attack, facing a race against time to ensure the safety of delegates at an upcoming international conference. Crime drama, guest starring Dan Lauria (The Wonder Years)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a Navy intelligence officer, and the choice of murder weapon leads the agents to believe he was killed by a colleague. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Navy Public Affairs Officer is killed, and when the team takes up the case, both the man's wife and his mistress emerge as potential suspects. Each woman offering a very different take on the chain of events that led to the murder, and the investigators are forced to decide which of the two is telling the truth. Meanwhile, DiNozzo's father comes to the aid of a homeless woman, who makes a surprising claim about his relationship with her. Guest starring Robert Wagner

Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Ralph Waite

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Mickey concludes a house fire was accidental until a message on the tenant's answer phone casts new light on the case. Crime Scene Examiner Eddie Olosunje confirms they are dealing with arson and his search of the property leads to further revelations. Back at the station Mickey questions the landlord and is stunned to learn the attractive solicitor making his life difficult is Banksy's wife

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: When a drink-driver injures a teenager in a hit-and-run incident, Sally and Ben assume the frantic woman giving a witness statement is the girl's mother. A more complicated picture of the relationship begins to emerge and the older woman's unpredictable behaviour leads to potential tragedy when she drives her car into the river. Gina loses control when Sally and Callum defy orders and try to save her from drowning

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A teacher is found unconscious and investigations reveal he has been involved in an affair with one of his pupils who has subsequently been registered as a missing person. A static signal from the girl's telephone alerts Tony and Sally to the fact they may be looking for a corpse. Arun and Millie's plan to catch the organisers of a spate of break-ins lands them in hot water

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Martin Jarvis guest stars as classic car dealer Leslie Downey who is injured when a scaffolding pole crashes into his vehicle, impaling him. As his life ebbs away he confides in Nikki, chatting about his business and his friendship with his passenger Anthony. Moved by the experience and suspecting the incident may have been a deliberate attack, Nikki and Will are forced to reassess their careers

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne receives an SOS from Holloway Prison, whose usual maternity workers have been struck down by illness, and when she and Trixie step in to help, the nun puts her reputation on the line to prevent a heavily pregnant convict having her unborn baby taken away by social services. Shelagh faces heartache when a routine pregnancy check reveals something far more serious, Alec has a confession for Jenny, and Fred takes the gang to the theatre to celebrate Chummy's birthday - only for the trip to end in disaster. Lacey Turner, best known as EastEnders' Stacey Branning, guest stars

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Jenny has an eventful few days, starting with a patient who as well as having herself to worry about, is also struggling to cope with her mother, an agoraphobic who hasn't been outside for at least 12 years. On top of that, Alec asks the midwife to accompany him to Brighton for the weekend, leaving her wondering if his intentions are strictly honourable. However, things soon take a tragic turn. Meanwhile, Shelagh joins the local choir to take her mind off her problems. Jessica Raine, Leo Staar and Laura Main star

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The nuns and midwives are busy preparing for Sister Evangelina's surprise jubilee celebrations, marking 50 years since she took her vows, but a face from the past threatens to ruin the party for the guest of honour. It's back to business when a young woman with Down's syndrome is brought in feeling unwell. To everyone's surprise, they discover she is six months pregnant, causing some of them to think the worst, and the staff are joined by new nurse Patsy, whose sense of humour and no-nonsense attitude make her an instant hit

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's A Dinner of Herbs
programme.desc: Conclusion of the two-part drama based on the novel by Catherine Cookson, starring Billie Whitelaw as a herbalist who takes an 11-year-old orphan under her wing. With Tim Healy, Jonathan Kerrigan and Michael Jayston

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: While the world anxiously awaits the resolution of the Cuban Missile Crisis, Valerie treats a pregnant Somali woman who has undergone female genital circumcision. The Nonnatus Team arrange for her to go into hospital, but she responds by going into hiding, and the situation is complicated further when it emerges that the woman's younger sister will soon go through the same process. Sister Mary Cynthia's depression is treated with electroconvulsive therapy, which she hopes will restore her faith

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length episode of the detective drama. Agatha Christie's elderly sleuth is intrigued by the case of a businessman who died in his 'counting house' with a pocketful of rye - and as she sets out to crack the 'crime by rhyme', suspicion soon falls on the household and staff at Yew Tree Lodge. Joan Hickson, Timothy West, Peter Davison, Tom Wilkinson, Stacy Dorning, Clive Merrison and Rachel Bell star

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Thomas performs a post mortem on a soldier who died in military training and his investigation takes a turn when he discovers traces of a possible nerve agent

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Eliza's other grandmother, Dinah, makes a dramatic entrance, but why is she really back?

programme.title: The Heart Guy

programme.desc: Sahira performs emergency surgery in the back of an ambulance, and the procedure provides Jac with the inspiration she needs to fight back against the plastics team. programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Nathan is persuaded to offer Harry an administrative position again, but the crafty manager has a trick up his sleeve, and offers him a role shared with Selena. Kelsey is left in a bad mood when the staff are banned from smoking at the hospital, until a chance meeting with a patient suffering with throat cancer leaves her to reconsider her habit, and Josh is angry when he finds the paramedics' rota has been changed behind his back

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Barney Hussein immediately becomes prime suspect when he is seen standing over the unconscious body of a woman in an alleyway and dropping a stun gun as he makes his escape. The confused victim reluctantly reveals a completely different train of events leading to the attack involving a flirtation with stripper Tom Norris. Will impresses Terry on his first day as a trainee Detective Constable with CID

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany finally gets some good news from Grant about getting a divorce. Pauline Fowler grows increasingly worried about Martin's behaviour

programme.desc: Ricky has to attend court and is shocked when he hears his sentence. Phil feels sorry for him and Ricky is worried about Bianca's reaction. Huw and Lenny decide it's time to confess

programme.desc: After Alex Grant's death, it emerges his daughter Rachel has been incarcerated in an asylum for 30 years for having an illegitimate child. Janet is determined to secure her release, but Dr Cameron doubts the wisdom of her efforts. Drama, starring David Rintoul, Ian Bannen and Annette Crosbie

programme.desc: Siegfried seizes an opportunity to show Callum his idea of the perfect operation, but his plans go awry, and James is in danger of missing his son's piano recital. Rural drama, starring Robert Hardy, Christopher Timothy and John McGlynn

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: While searching a Cambridge antiques fair for religious books with Charlotte and Charlie Gimbert, Lovejoy discovers a rare Bible that seems to offer a solution to all his problems. Meanwhile, Tinker has a hard time persuading Beth's father that she should carry on working for such a rogue. Drama, guest starring Richard Briers and James Ellis, with Ian McShane

programme.title: French Fields
programme.desc: While William prepares for the first day of his new job, Hester faces the prospect of dealing with a French plumber and is later caught off-guard by an unexpected visitor. Comedy, with Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.desc: Diana takes matters into her own hands, asking Tom for sleeping pills after falling ill and fearing the worst. Stephanie Cole stars

programme.desc: Daddy has an accident while chasing a milkwoman on his bicycle, and ends up heavily sedated in hospital. First-ever episode of the suburban sitcom, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and George Webb

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Lovestruck Truly learns his childhood sweetheart is to return from Australia - and eager to make a good impression, immediately sets about making sure the long-awaited expat receives an unforgettable welcome. Rural comedy, starring Frank Thornton and Bill Owen

programme.title: The Coroner
programme.desc: The reopening of a local restaurant ends abruptly the owner is shot dead by a sniper. Davey takes an early lead on the case but is soon undermined by the arrival of DI Ben Marshall from the Metropolitan Police, who not only takes over his investigation but also takes a shine to Jane. Crime drama, starring Claire Goose and Matt Bardock

programme.title: Luther
programme.desc: A spate of murders occurs across London, and Luther suspects they were committed by the same villain. However, hundreds of hours of CCTV footage and a huge pool of witnesses seems to point to the contrary. Meanwhile, Henry Madsen wakes from his coma, giving the detective reason to fear for his career - and Alice's life. Idris Elba stars

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The detectives investigate the kidnapping of two boys in 1979, which they believe could be linked to a recent spate of disappearances. The youngsters involved in the earlier case escaped - though their abductor was never found. Fearing they may be on the trail of a serial killer, the detectives also uncover a link with another unsolved case - the assault on a champion fisherman

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Nikki is humiliated in court when a barrister accuses her of making a serious error in the case of a policeman who may or may not have been murdered. Meanwhile, the Lintern team investigates the death of a medical researcher, whose body was found in a canal with mysterious puncture marks on it. Long-running crime drama following the cases of a team of forensic pathology experts, starring David Caves, Emilia Fox, Richard Lintern and Liz Carr, with Art Malik and Dervla Kirwan

programme.desc: A joyrider makes her escape, leaving Millie and Nate to discover a body in the boot of the abandoned car. programme.desc: Ricky and Bianca's wedding is only one day away - but all's not well between them. Tony gets his first big break and Nigel is off to see an old friend

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Lynley and Havers investigate a case at Smithfield Market when a frozen body is found on a meat truck. The victim is carrying a page of parchment written in Arabic and a counterfeit British passport. A number of suspects emerge, including a dealer in antiquities, a forger, and a surgeon who may have been selling human organs. With Nathaniel Parker, Sharon Small and Mark Benton

programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Chelsea at Etihad Stadium and Liverpool v Southampton at Anfield. City and Liverpool were both out for revenge as the Citizens had lost 1-0 to Chelsea in last month's FA Cup semi-final and the Reds were defeated 1-0 by Southampton in January's reverse fixture. Plus, Leeds United v Tottenham Hotspur and Sheffield United v Crystal Palace

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt hosts a special celebration of the Muslim festival of Eid al-Fitr, joined by chefs Asma Khan and Shelina Permalloo, who share recipes for once Ramadan comes to an end

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Wolverhampton Wanderers v Brighton & Hove Albion (Kick-off 12.00pm). All the action from the Premier League encounter at Molineux. Both sides are likely to be disappointed with their seasons, but they put on a show in the reverse fixture at Amex Stadium in January, with the match ending in a thrilling 3-3 draw, with Wolves leading 3-2 at the end of frenetic first half, before Lewis Dunk gave the Seagulls a share of the points with a strike 20 minutes from time

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps an intrepid couple search for a home in Somerset with a budget of £900,000, complete with space for a helipad

programme.title: Inside Out (2015)
programme.desc: Inside 11-year-old Riley's head lives a team of five that controls her emotions. Led by the eternally upbeat Joy, they have always ensured she lived a happy life - but when Riley's parents move to a new city, she is plunged into depression and the emotions face a crisis that threatens their entire world. Pixar animated fantasy, with the voices of Amy Poehler and Phyllis Smith

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates footage of emperor penguins in Atka Bay, on the coast of Antarctica, as they return from three months of feeding at sea to raise their chicks. They arrive as the sea freezes over and other animals desert the area for safer territory - but if the penguins are to nurture a future generation, they will have to work together to endure some of the harshest conditions on Earth

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison explore Cranborne Chase, help tend to Britain's biggest modern-day maze, and meet farmers who are working together to encourage wildlife on their land. Steve Brown goes stargazing, Tom Heap reports on the possible environmental costs of a new space race launched from rural sites, and Adam Henson takes his rare breed Suffolk Punch to the maternity unit

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from the shores of Lake Windermere in the Lake District, and discovers the stories of children liberated from concentration camps who were brought to the area to start a new life. Items brought in to be valued include a Cherokee pipe, a wooden decoy duck, a First World War portrait, and a piece of footballing history

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The nation is in the grip of World Cup fever in 1966, and Fred has placed a risky accumulator bet on the outcome of the final which he starts to regret as the big match draws near. Nonnatus House awaits the arrival of four new pupil midwives, Dr Turner and Nurse Crane discover a 17-year-old boy is harbouring a desperate secret, and Cyril and Lucille's relationship continues to blossom

programme.title: The Pursuit of Love
programme.desc: Adaptation of Nancy Mitford's novel about two cousins living in 1920s Oxfordshire. Linda dreams of a life of adventure, while Fanny is more concerned with stability after being abandoned by her mother. When a glamorous local aristocrat invites a charming stranger to a ball, Linda is instantly smitten, but Fanny's disapproval threatens to tear the inseparable friends apart. With Lily James, Emily Beecham, Dominic West, Freddie Fox and Andrew Scott. Written and directed by Emily Mortimer, who co-stars as Fanny's absent mother

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by studio guests to review the day's Premier League action, including Aston Villa v Manchester United at Villa Park. United were looking to emulate their 2-1 triumph over the Villans at Old Trafford in January and keep their faint hopes of winning the title alive. Plus, Arsenal v West Bromwich Albion, West Ham United v Everton and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Brighton & Hove Albion

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Chelsea v Reading, West Ham United v Manchester City and Arsenal v Aston Villa

programme.title: Ian Wright: Home Truths
programme.desc: The footballer-turned-broadcaster investigates what effect growing up in a psychologically abusive and violent home has on children in the UK. He meets with social workers to find out how they are supporting and protecting youngsters living in at-risk households during Covid-19 as well as adults and young people who have been affected by growing up with domestic abuse. Ian also shares the story of his own formative years, shaped by a difficult relationship with his stepfather, and on the final leg of his journey visits a charity who work with people at risk of committing, or who have already committed, domestic abuse

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Angela Rippon and Julia Somerville report on gardens that have been branded dangerous after a landslide, deceptive locksmiths, and home insurance small print

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A period one-bedroom cottage near Blackwell in Worcestershire, a huge Methodist chapel in Dover in Kent and a three-bedroom end-of-terrace in Bootle on Merseyside are all sold to new, ambitious owners. Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow progress as they get underway to make their property dreams a reality

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: The cases of a council worker who spent £200,000 of public funds on horse riding and holidays, and a woman who pretended she could barely move to falsely claim benefits

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Mountain Rescue comes to the aid of a couple on a mountaineering date in the Lake District. Plus, a teenage boy jumps into a water-filled dyke to save a drowning horse

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents the show from Shrewsbury, and learns more about the world's most famous phone box. Thomas Forrester and Kate Bliss are on hand to help the teams

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie gets excited by her first day of HCA training, and is then surprised when Nathan turns up at the Mill. Jimmi gets drawn into a dispute between a brother and sister

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple to find a new home in Somerset, and visits a shopkeeper who is encouraging customers to think about their shopping habits in a bid to reduce waste

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore teams up with blacksmith Kev Paxton and artisan Leigh-Anne Treadwell to transform an iron table base, a tub chair and a wooden till box

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine and David Harper hunt for antiques in Lincolnshire and Hull, coming back with a door knocker, a chocolate elephant mould, and a pair of opera glasses

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the studio, with the usual topical stories and celebrity chat. Presented by Alex Jones and new full-time hosts Ronan Keating and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Behind the scenes with Kent police tackling the drugs trade, and the 'county lines' set up to distribute narcotics bought in London across the county - often using children and vulnerable people as carriers. The programme examines how the illegal drugs trade has continued to flourish despite the limitations placed on movement during lockdown

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Pre-wedding jitters are at an all-time high as Callum faces the possibility that Ben will stand him up at the altar - and with Kathy thinking the same thing, she encourages Phil to speak to their son. Zack tries to help Nancy but his heroic plan does not go well, and Keegan panics when he finds Bailey's dog Banjo, alone and injured

programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: Basil goes to great lengths to prove to Sybil that a guest has smuggled a woman into his room. Instead the hotelier keeps being caught in compromising situations with a young Australian woman, and a visiting psychiatrist finds his increasingly manic behaviour both fascinating and alarming. Classic comedy first broadcast in 1979, starring John Cleese and Prunella Scales

programme.title: Three Families
programme.desc: Part one of two. Drama about the campaign to relax abortion laws in Northern Ireland, from the point of view of three families who find themselves coming into conflict with the law. A mother realises she could face a prison sentence for helping her teenage daughter to get an abortion, while a newlywed discovers her unborn baby will not survive to the end of her pregnancy - but also that she will legally still have to carry the child to term

programme.title: Starstruck
programme.desc: Kate and Jessie's murder mystery party is interrupted by the arrival of Tom, in need of help. Jessie struggles to juggle her multiple love interests

programme.title: Eating with My Ex
programme.desc: Amy wants to know if the reason ex Marney never made them official was because he was actually cheating on her. Lucy wants to find out why ex Ethan was sliding into her mates DMs when they were together. Steve thought he had found love in Amira, until she ghosted him and moved on. Now reunited for the first time, Amira reveals the shocking truth that could end them for good

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race Down Under
programme.desc: Superstar sisters Kylie and Dannii Minogue will be joining RuPaul and regular judges Michelle Visage and Rhys Nicholson for the second round of the contest, in which Australia and New Zealand's finest drag queens dress up for the Celebrity Impression Olympics

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Louise Redknapp, as they assist a brother and sister from Stamford in picking out the best singers from a line-up that includes a singing student and a personal trainer

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford meets victims of a scam that's been sweeping the UK, starting with a simple text message about parcel deliveries before leading people into a terrifyingly plausible confidence trick. Plus, how some customers of one buy-and-sell website, designed to turn stash into cash, were left exposed to fraudsters

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the progress of semi-detached properties in Sneyd Green in Stoke on Trent and Low Hill in Wolverhampton

programme.title: State Opening of Parliament
programme.desc: Huw Edwards presents live coverage of the Queen's speech from Westminster, as Her Majesty outlines the government's plans for the coming year ahead of the first session of the new parliament

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Mark Stacey help two teams find items of value at Harrogate antiques fair, scouring the event in the hope that they will make a profit at auction. Amidst the highs and lows in the saleroom, presenter Anita Manning manages to find time to also embark on a quilting quest at a local museum

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie helps Nathan when he struggles to go through with meeting his birth mother. Ruhma is baffled by a young couple's approach to choosing their baby's defining characteristics

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Contestants Darren, Kerry, Mustafa and Gugu vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to win a cash prize. Quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a London couple moving to Surrey, hoping their £900,000 budget finds them a large home that is close to good schools and with easy links to the capital

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph enlists furniture restorer Katie Keat to repair a pair of wooden chairs and woodworker Carwyn Owen finds a creative solution for an unwanted pine wardrobe

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: David Harper buys an old fishing stool and Arusha Irvine splashes out on a parachuting clown as they travel through York and Durham. But only one can win at the Newcastle auction

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda is shocked at the sight of Zack in her home but when the intruder lets slip that Nancy wants to move out, she and Mick soon have something much more important to worry about. Now they have found Banjo, the Taylors prepare to step up their search for Bailey - but then Mitch receives a phone call about his daughter

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Donna leads the Holby nursing staff in protest - will Hanssen be moved by their cause or are things about to get much worse for them? Lucky realises that Kian lied to her about where Andrei was staying, while an accidental encounter between Josh and Hanssen threatens to spill both of their secrets

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste look into the case of widow Jaqueline, who ventured online to look for love, only to meet a man who targeted her life savings, stealing a six-figure sum before disappearing. However, several years on a solicitor has been in touch, claiming he has died and left Jaqueline money in his estate. Is this a genuine inheritance or just the latest stage of a sickening scam?

programme.title: Three Families
programme.desc: Concluding the two-part drama about the campaign to relax abortion laws in Northern Ireland. Hannah is determined to change the law and throws herself into campaigning - catapulting her into the centre of the debate. Theresa learns her trial date, but still has not told Orla that she is facing prison for what they did, and first-time older mother Rosie discovers there is no legal remedy to the complications with her pregnancy. Starring Amy James-Kelly, Sinead Keenan and Genevieve O'Reilly

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The hopefuls meet up at the Selfie Factory in London's O2 for an assignment set in the lucrative world of social media, which sees them creating fun and impactful looks on themselves to be posted on the global video-sharing platform TikTok. But as well as the make-up, they must also film and edit a video - all within the space of two hours. Back in the studio, the online theme continues as they create a look based on a face filter

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Bossin' It
programme.desc: In March 2020, university student Ellis Lloyd Jones, locked-down and bored, began turning out more and more of the TikTok videos he'd started posting five months earlier. Soon his posts began to go viral. Here, he reveals the truth behind his success. Laura Mallows reveals the meteoric and unusual rise of her vegan beauty products company Mallows Beauty. Last spring, the pandemic came as a big blow to her fledgling business, but with presence of mind Laura came up with a way out of her predicament as she became one of the first people in the UK to apply for a licence to manufacture hand-sanitizer

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman's relationship with Norma becomes strained as he grows closer to Emma. Meanwhile, Romero meets his competition for the re-elections. Thriller, starring Vera Farmiga

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Romero investigates two mysterious deaths in White Pine Bay. As Norman and Emma spend time away from the motel, buried secrets start to emerge for Caleb and Dylan, and Norma returns to school. Thriller, starring Freddie Highmore

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin visits a dedicated group of gardeners in Grimsby who are planning to brighten up a neglected stretch of river that runs through one of the most deprived estates in town

programme.title: The Edible Garden
programme.desc: Alys Fowler demonstrates how easy it is to grow raspberries and blueberries, and plants juicy fruits from which she then prepares jams and tangy fruit leathers. She also tries her hand at hapazome, a Japanese printing technique used to transfer flower and leaf shapes on to fabric

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: In Adam Frost's garden, he gives his tips on lawn repair, grows some unusual vegetables and plants a seasonal container. Meanwhile, Advolly Richmond brings us up to date in her history of lawns

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: John Craven celebrates 50 years as a BBC presenter and his 1250th episode of Countryfile by revisiting parts of the country that hold special memories for him. He uncovers hidden treasures in his childhood Yorkshire home, learns about peasant poet John Clare in Cambridgeshire, and visits an ancient Scottish battlefield

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond heads to Kelso, to celebrate the 250th anniversary of literary great and Borders boy Walter Scott and explore the landscape that inspired him. Arlene Stuart meets a modern day farmer and author Patrick Laurie, who is encouraging other rural writers to 'get stuck in', while Euan McIlwraith points the focus on one of his favourite birds, the eider duck. Plus, the first of a new series of features about farms cutting their environmental impact sees Anne Lundon pay a visit to a dairy farm that has ditched soya in favour of a home-grown feed

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter and Mairi Rattray show how to create a bluebell wood and also lay ready-sown turf to create a near-instant carpet of colour. In sunny Joppa, George Anderso has the results of some of his pruning exploits from last year, while Kirsty Wilson is back in her allotment creating a cold frame from scratch using recycled bits and pieces

programme.title: A Farewell to Arms (1932)
programme.desc: An American army lieutenant falls in love with a British nurse while serving in the First World War. A jealous rival strives to keep them apart as the officer is sent to the front line in Italy, but a series of twists of fate continually throw them back together. Romantic drama based on Ernest Hemingway's novel, starring Gary Cooper and Helen Hayes

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Profiles of handsome heroes and pin-ups of the silver screen, including Paul Newman, Robert Redford, Jane Russell and Marilyn Monroe. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from the North West go head-to-head cooking their entire six-course menus for the judges, including Wayne Hemingway MBE. Andi Oliver hosts

programme.title: Sunday Morning Stories
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley hears from people who have used their religion and self-belief to overcome adversity in their lives, including a couple determined to not let their different faiths be a barrier to them getting married

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Brenda Edwards visits Swansea to explore the Welsh Revival, when Christianity spread rapidly through Wales, and discovers a new church reviving an old chapel today

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers have their say on the BBC's output and there are behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Author and hunter Kenny Salwey, who lives off the land and enjoys being close to nature, presents a guide to the wildlife of the Mississippi river, including beavers, snapping turtles, sturgeon, pelicans and eagles

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: David Harper and Christina Trevanion value items brought into Aberystwyth University's Old College, while Paul Martin visits Laugharne, the home of his literary hero Dylan Thomas

programme.title: All That Glitters: Britain's Next Jewellery Star
programme.desc: Katherine Ryan hosts as the five remaining contestants are challenged to make a pair of identical gold hoop earrings, before creating a gold locket for a real-world client. Who will impress the judges enough to stay to make it through to the semi-final?

programme.title: Catch Me If You Can (2002)
programme.desc: The story of Frank Abagnale Jr, who found notoriety as a teenager conning and charming his way across America. Posing as a pilot, a doctor and a lawyer, Frank was able to fund an extravagant lifestyle while outwitting the FBI at every turn. Fact-based drama, starring Leonardo DiCaprio, Tom Hanks, Christopher Walken, Martin Sheen and James Brolin

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Documentary following life on farms around Scotland, from Argyll to the Outer Hebrides. The Rodgers face a final TB test. Animals arrive at Lynn and Sandra's croft. Raymond is kept guessing by his surrogate sheep and a calving boom starts at Kirsty and Aimee's farm

programme.title: Our Wild Adventures
programme.desc: BBC wildlife presenters and crew take a trip through the natural history archives, beginning by sharing some of their most memorable animal encounters across Africa. Gordon Buchanan reminisces about the start of his career when he was 17, Chris Packham looks back on of his adventures in Kenya, and Steve Backshall shares a story about diving with Nile crocodiles in the Okavango delta of Botswana

programme.title: Gods of Snooker
programme.desc: The sport's rise to prominence, beginning by examining how Alex 'Hurricane' Higgins upset the snooker establishment to help transform a game played in the backrooms of working men's clubs into a TV ratings hit. Interest in the sport had been growing thanks to the new possibilities of colour broadcasting, and in particular the weekly snooker show Pot Black, but it was the antics of the unpredictable Ulstersman that took the game stratospheric

programme.title: Alex Higgins: The People's Champion
programme.desc: James Nesbitt narrates a look back at the often controversial life of the two-time world snooker champion, who was widely regarded as one of the most naturally gifted players in the history of his sport, but whose brilliance was flawed by his demons. With contributions by members of his family, as well as fellow snooker greats Jimmy White, Ronnie O'Sullivan, Steve Davis and Dennis Taylor

programme.title: Snowfall
programme.desc: Franklin attempts to stop a gang war from exploding as the violence in South Central ramps up, while reporter Irene Abe tries to understand what's happening to her neighbourhood. The return of the drama about the beginnings of the crack epidemic in Los Angeles. Starring Damson Idris

programme.title: Snowfall
programme.desc: Chaos reigns as Franklin's plans go awry and he turns to an old flame for help, and while Leon struggles without him, Cissy takes a more active role in his business. Gustavo's world is shaken and Irene finds her way to Alton's shelter

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: With Noah's life hanging in the balance, Peter and the others unite to try to prevent Samuel killing thousands of people. Final-ever episode of the drama, starring Jack Coleman and Milo Ventimiglia

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: The cause of Reyhan's death prompts an investigation, putting Hanssen and Sahira's already brittle relationship at breaking point. The success of Dominic's operation gives him a new-found sense of hope for the future, while Kian continues to lie to Lucky about notifying social services regarding Andrei

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood works as a roadside mechanic, finding out what it takes to keep the UK's motorists moving. He follows breakdowns from the initial alert, learns techniques for roadside assistance and rescues stranded motorists with one of the largest automotive service companies in the country

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from the Ardingly International Antiques Fair, West Sussex, with experts Philip Serrell and Roo Irvine ready to help the two teams as they vie to see who can buy the most profitable three items to take to auction. Charlie also visits a family business that specialises in conserving historical artifacts

programme.title: Inside the Zoo
programme.desc: Vets at Edinburgh Zoo deal with a zebra with arthritis, while a binturong is being prepared to leave for the south of France as part of a breeding programme, and keepers Vickie and Laura try to weigh a polar bear

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc in which pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords, in an attempt to take home £2,500 in cash. As the teams solve the passwords, they win letters that will help them later in the game, giving them a better chance of cracking the final password which unlocks the Letterbox holding the cash prize. However, if they end up as the losing pair along the way, they'll have to surrender all the letters they've won to the winning pair - who will then go through to the semi-final eliminator round

programme.title: Good Morning Dagenham
programme.desc: Documentary following traders at a Sunday market A new couple have an unusual product to sell, and the market crew puts on a fashion show

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: Still upset after her confrontation with Katie, Shay avoids the Turner household. Poppy faces bullying on social media before the school ball, and the election for president of the New Republic of Weld heats up. Comedy drama, starring Erik Thomson

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode's alphabetical journey through the BBC's culinary archive continues as he reaches the letter K. Highlights include Rick Stein cooking lamb koftas and Raymond Blanc serving up kougelhopf

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman hosts the return of the competition in which 10 home cooks move into a shared house and compete in a series of weekly challenges designed to demonstrate their flair, skill and creativity. There is also a new addition to the judging line-up as Michelin-starred chef Angela Hartnett joins home cooking queen Mary Berry and produce expert Chris Bavin. For their first two challenges, the cooks have to come up with their ultimate pasta dinner and a dish based on a piece of fruit

programme.title: Nature's Microworlds
programme.desc: Steve Backshall examines life in Australia's Red Centre, a desert landscape in the Outback, where two-metre tall kangaroos, burrowing toads and the world's most venomous snakes are among the throng of animals that live there. He also looks at how, in a place that is parched by the sun, scorched by fire and prone to unpredictable floods, creatures successfully make the heart of this island continent their home

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A collection of finds from various locations, including Lulworth Castle in Dorset, Chiddingstone Castle in Kent and Wrest Park in Bedfordshire. Presenter Paul Martin also goes on a tour of the National Motor Museum at Beaulieu in Hampshire

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Previous winners Chizzy Akudolu, Miles Jupp, Amol Rajan and Ellie Taylor go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo visits Crewe, a town steeped in railway history, to investigate the making of cinema classic The Night Mail during the 1930s. He then heads to Chester to discover the interwar origins of Britain's most popular zoo, then goes across the border to the Welsh village of Gresford, scene of one of Britain's worst mining disasters

programme.title: Nigellissima
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson presents a guide to Italian cookery, demonstrating how to create traditional and exciting meals using ingredients that are widely available in most supermarkets. She begins by preparing Sicilian pasta with tomatoes, garlic and almonds, a family feast of tagliata and Tuscan fries, and a fiery egg dish designed for late-night parties

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Sara Cox returns with the programme in which famous faces each bring with them their favourite book of all time to discuss with their fellow guests. In the first edition she is joined by Mel Giedroyc, Griff Rhys Jones, Oti Mabuse and Rick Edwards, who reviewing this week's two book club picks - The Vanishing Half by Brit Bennett and The Fine Art of Invisible Detection by Robert Goddard

programme.title: The Great Northern Garden Build
programme.desc: Documentary following the creation of RHS Garden Bridgewater in Salford, one of the most ambitious and challenging gardening projects the UK has ever seen. The programme goes behind the scenes to meet the team of designers, engineers and gardeners taking on this colossal construction project on the site of a former grand Victorian estate in Greater Manchester

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: As an outbreak of headlice sweeps the school, Julia finds herself accused of triggering a second wave. Ostracised by the other mums, she needs to find a way back into their good books and decides to throw a nit treatment party at her home. The gathering reveals that Anne has some big news, Meg is facing a crisis, Kevin has committed a terrible crime of passion and Liz is waiting to hear about a possible career move. The return of the comedy, starring Anna Maxwell Martin and Diane Morgan

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith's darkly comic anthology returns. Pantalone is planning the heist of the century - a tasty £12million in rough-cut diamonds. But if you're going to hire a bunch of complete clowns to carry out a robbery, and half of them are planning to double-cross the other half, you've not just got a recipe for disaster, you've actually got the ingredients of a Tarantino Commedia dell'Arte. Starring Kevin Bishop, Gemma Whelan and Paterson Joseph

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Steph McGovern hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with comedian Zoe Lyons and journalist John Pienaar joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: The First Team
programme.desc: Comedy written by Iain Morris and Damon Beesley - creators of The Inbetweeners - following the adventures of three young footballers, their Italian manager and eccentric chairman. New signing Mattie Sullivan arrives at the club, but a meeting with chairman Mark Crane leads him to wonder if he's been signed by mistake. As if that wasn't bad enough, club hard-man Petey Brooks takes a dislike to him and legendary Italian manager Cesare is nowhere to be found. Starring Shaquille Ali-Yebuah, Will Arnett, Jack McMullen, Jake Short and Theo Barklem-Biggs

programme.title: The First Team
programme.desc: Mattie befriends star midfielder Carlos Velez in an attempt to improve team spirit. But hanging out with Carlos is not quite what he imagined. Jack braves a date with a girl he met online, Benji is approached by a mysterious businessman and Petey plays pranks on the squad. Football comedy from the creators of The Inbetweeners, starring Shaquille Ali-Yebuah, Jack McMullen, Jake Short and Theo Barklem-Biggs

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: John Craven celebrates 50 years as a BBC presenter and his 1250th episode of Countryfile by revisiting parts of the country that hold special memories for him. He uncovers hidden treasures in his childhood Yorkshire home, learns about peasant poet John Clare in Cambridgeshire, and visits an ancient Scottish battlefield

programme.title: Different League: The Derry City Story
programme.desc: Documentary about how football club Derry City made a comeback in 1985 after withdrawing from the Irish League in 1972, when they were accepted to play in the League of Ireland. Featuring interviews with Tony O'Doherty, Terry Harkin, Eddie Mahon and Eamonn McLaughlin, who were known as the Gang of Four and were the men behind the club's rebirth, as well as rare archive footage and memories of faithful supporters

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from the shores of Lake Windermere in the Lake District, and discovers the stories of children liberated from concentration camps who were brought to the area to start a new life. Items brought in to be valued include some rare Dinky toys, a First World War portrait, and a piece of footballing history

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore teams up with blacksmith Kev Paxton and artisan Leigh-Anne Treadwell to transform an iron table base, a tub chair and a wooden till box

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents the show from Shrewsbury, and learns more about the world's most famous phone box. Thomas Forrester and Kate Bliss are on hand to help the teams

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don give a masterclass on growing Mediterranean herbs, plants lily bulbs for summer scent, and in the vegetable garden, he begins to sow seeds for salad root crops

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the progress of terraced houses in Newcastle-under-Lyme and Offerton as they are sold at auction

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: The investigators go on the hunt for a manager treating the NHS like a cash machine, and a headteacher is exposed as a cheat, then taught a lesson

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A young man lies unconscious in his bedroom and it is up to an on-line friend in Texas to raise the alarm. Plus, a nine-year-old boy on holiday is stung by a giant jellyfish

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc, as pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords, in an attempt to take home £2,500 in cash

programme.title: Good Morning Dagenham
programme.desc: The market crew puts on its first dog show, a first-timer comes to the market creating stories for the passing public, and a meat auctioneer put his reluctant sidekick centre stage

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: George and Fiona confront Big Mac about his plan to change Weld. With Shay going back to school and staying with her father, Katie rents a tiny house-truck, and Poppy's mother comes to visit Woody and Tracey. Comedy drama, starring Erik Thomson

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode continues his alphabetical tour through the BBC's culinary archive, this time selecting recipes and ingredients linked by the letter H. Tony Allan and Giorgio Locatelli cook ham bone soup and Jamie Oliver makes a pine nut and honey tart

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Mary Berry asks the contestants to produce their ultimate meringue dessert for a family celebration lunch - leading to stunning pavlovas, inventive fruit curd pies and even a woodland-themed roulade. This is followed by Chris Bavin's one-hour challenge to rustle up a dish from scratch featuring a humble vegetable as the star of the show, before the judges decide who has to take on Angela Hartnett's elimination cook for their place in the contest - a single recipe that requires precision timing and perfect seasoning. Claudia Winkleman hosts

programme.title: Nature's Microworlds
programme.desc: Steve Backshall visits the Highlands, home to some of the most iconic wildlife in the British Isles, including red squirrels and mountain hares. The two contrasting landscapes of open moor and Caledonian forest are equally important to the animals that live there, and yet the history of the area shows that they should not exist side by side. He also reveals the part that humans have to play in allowing both habitats to thrive

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Highlights of the best finds by experts Anita Manning, Mark Stacey and others during the programme's travels around the country. Items featured include a set of Beatles autographs and a collection of Matchbox toys. Plus, presenter Paul Martin visits the birthplace of novelist and playwright DH Lawrence

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Chizzy Akudolu, Miles Jupp, Amol Rajan and Ellie Taylor go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's 1930s Bradshaw's-inspired railway tour continues in north Wales. In Colwyn Bay, Michael hears how an army of civil servants from the Ministries of Food and Agriculture in London invaded the quiet seaside town, before visiting an amusement park in the holiday resort of Rhyl. Crossing the Menai Strait by the Britannia Bridge, Michael arrives on Anglesey and the elegant seat of the Marquesses of the island

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads to Berlin, where history and the avant garde dwell side by side. A city that once made history with its divisive wall and Checkpoint Charlie now teems with an overwhelming array of innovative chefs offering delicious seasonal creations, such as pureed sunflower seeds, Jerusalem artichoke flans and crushed frozen pine nuts. Loved by the likes of Greta Garbo, Charlie Chaplin and Auguste Escoffier there's still a cornucopia of choice for those with a more traditional temperament with eisbein, meatballs and sausages topping every menu in the German capital

programme.title: All That Glitters: Britain's Next Jewellery Star
programme.desc: Katherine Ryan hosts as the last four contestants have to make a cuff bracelet, then attempt to create an engagement ring for couple James and Clare, before judges Shaun Leane and Solange Azagury-Partridge decide who to put through to the final

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The return of the documentary following the day-to-day realities the NHS faces in unprecedented times, capturing it in the year spent reacting to the pandemic. With nearly five million people now waiting for treatment in England alone, doctors are forced to take incredibly difficult decisions as they battle to save lives and fight for patients to have the treatment they desperately need

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Guests Miles Jupp, Deirdre O'Kane and Phill Jupitus join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme 'non sequiturs' in this extended episode. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Saved By a Stranger
programme.desc: Cameras focus on Marc, a public health worker who was diagnosed with HIV aged 17 in the late 1980s. He found support at the Landmark Centre in Brixton via a volunteer called John. Nearly 30 years later, Marc is desperate to thank John for helping him. Plus, Belfast dad Peter searches for a rehabilitation nurse called Betsy who helped him recover from a gunshot wound during the Troubles

programme.title: Bent Coppers: Crossing the Line of Duty
programme.desc: The final instalment reveals how, in the late 1970s, corruption within the City of London's CID had grown out of control - and how Operation Countryman was launched in response

programme.title: Greta Thunberg: A Year to Change the World
programme.desc: The Covid-19 pandemic reveals the enormity of the challenge that climate change poses, as the drop in emissions during lockdown proves far from enough to get the world back on track. She explores new measures to reduce the level of emissions further, visiting a tree plantation and learning about a machine that sucks carbon dioxide out of the air. She also explains the everyday choices that members of the public can make to reduce their environmental impact

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the Fowey Estuary, one of Cornwall's hidden secrets, joining a friend on a boat trip to learn about the history of the area and getting a unique view of the buildings along the riverbank. He discovers the area's history of piracy, meets a family that make award-winning vodka, and makes a pancake with a wild blackberry compote and Cornish clotted cream

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Revolution and Romance: Musical Masters of the 19th Century
programme.desc: Suzy Klein investigates how music became a powerful force for Europe's citizens during the French Revolution. She reveals how music came to express not only revolutionary fervour, but also inspired a growing sense of nationalism that was sweeping the continent. Chopin's music, for example, expressed the defiant spirit of the Polish population suffering oppression under a foreign power

programme.title: Britain's Pompeii: A Village Lost in Time
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts joins the team excavating a 3,000-year-old Bronze Age village in the Cambridgeshire Fens that burned to the ground and was preserved in peat. Archaeologists also investigate the cause of the fire. Was it just a terrible accident, or did the villagers' wealth provoke an attack?

programme.title: The Windermere Children: In Their Own Words
programme.desc: The story of the pioneering project to rehabilitate child survivors of the Holocaust on the shores of Lake Windermere, with first-hand testimonies from some of those who attended. Reflecting on the survivors' lives after Windermere, the film includes home-movie footage and footage of their annual reunions

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The team behind the Gaia space telescope, which is being used to build the most accurate star map of the galaxy ever created, as well as revealing what changes the future may hold

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: The scientific story behind the moment the first stars appeared in the universe, known as the Cosmic Dawn, which transformed the dark world originally created by the Big Bang. Astronomers now have the tools to explore this dramatic event that created the first ingredients of life as we know it, when the first stars were born to light up the universe. With contributions by Professor Tom Abel, Professor Volker Bromm, Charmaine Green and Margaret Whitehurst

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Mindfulness expert Andy Puddicombe reveals why breathing is so important to becoming still and being in the moment, illustrated with natural history footage

programme.title: The Violence Paradox
programme.desc: Psychologist Steven Pinker journeys through history and the human mind to explore what triggers violence and how it may have decreased over time. In the first of two programmes, research includes clues from a prehistoric archaeological dig in Kenya, an experiment to test the moral judgement of babies and data analysis of 239 years of Old Bailey proceedings

programme.title: The Violence Paradox
programme.desc: In the second of the two-part documentary, psychologist Steven Pinker examines evidence that suggests the world is becoming more peaceful, including how income equality and personal contact through sport may curb violence in modern societies

programme.title: Britain's Pompeii: A Village Lost in Time
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts joins the team excavating a 3,000-year-old Bronze Age village in the Cambridgeshire Fens that burned to the ground and was preserved in peat. Archaeologists also investigate the cause of the fire. Was it just a terrible accident, or did the villagers' wealth provoke an attack?

programme.title: Revolution and Romance: Musical Masters of the 19th Century
programme.desc: Suzy Klein investigates how music became a powerful force for Europe's citizens during the French Revolution. She reveals how music came to express not only revolutionary fervour, but also inspired a growing sense of nationalism that was sweeping the continent. Chopin's music, for example, expressed the defiant spirit of the Polish population suffering oppression under a foreign power

programme.title: The Pennine Way
programme.desc: Adventurer Paul Rose pulls on his hiking boots to explore the 268-mile Pennine Way, to mark the 50th anniversary of Britain's first national trail being officially opened. His first leg takes him from Edale to Calderdale, and along the way he tells the story of Tom Stephenson, who fought landowners and governments to win public access to the full route. He also meets author and director Barrie Rutter, who recalls some of the literary greats who have lived nearby, including Ted Hughes and the Brontes

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a skyscape depicting sunlight breaking through from behind clouds in shades of crimson and gold

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate more potentially valuable works of art. They begin with an 18th-century landscape that could be a lost work by of one of the biggest names in British art, Thomas Gainsborough. The painting has been in the family of owner Mark Cropper for generations and until the 1970s it was considered to be a Gainsborough. But then a valuation downgraded it and Mark's father tore off the Gainsborough label in disgust. Now Mark would love to be able to re-apply the Gainsborough plaque

programme.title: Gettys: The World's Richest Art Dynasty
programme.desc: Documentary looking at the cultural impact of the Gettys, who speak about their personal milestones in art collecting and philanthropy. They also discuss some of the family nightmares that have shaped common public perceptions of them, most infamously the kidnapping of John Paul Getty III in 1973

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Mindfulness expert Andy Puddicombe shows what can be learned from the ways in which animals adapt to changes in the world around them

programme.title: Danceworks
programme.desc: The return of the documentary offering an insight into the world of modern and classical dance. The first edition follows Strictly Come Dancing professionals Gorka Marquez and Karen Hauer as they rehearse choreography for a new Latin dance spectacular

programme.title: Danceworks
programme.desc: Following Sharon Eyal and Gai Behar create, rehearse and premiere a new dance, performed on the top of a multistorey car park in Peckham

programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a skyscape depicting sunlight breaking through from behind clouds in shades of crimson and gold

programme.title: The Pennine Way
programme.desc: Paul Rose's continuing journey takes him into the Yorkshire Dales, where 50 years ago the Pennine Way was launched in the village of Malham. Paul hears from those who remember the opening ceremony, before pulling on his climbing gear to attempt the limestone cliff face of Malham Cove. He also visits a sheep sale in Wensleydale and enjoys a pint in Britain's highest pub

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross has more painting tips as he captures on canvas an untouched forest containing an old country house, perched on a cliff and hidden in foliage

programme.title: Yes Minister
programme.desc: A conflict of interests arises at the Ministry of Administrative Affairs when Hacker introduces controversial cost-cutting measures. Satirical comedy, starring Paul Eddington and Nigel Hawthorne

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: Audrey moves out of Grantleigh Manor, and Richard and his mother move in, disappointing the former resident with their new ways. Comedy, starring Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles

programme.title: Double Cross: The True Story of the D-Day Spies
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre brings his best-selling book to the small screen, revealing the true story of five of the double agents who helped to make D-Day such a success. As well as being a victory of arms and a tactical coup, the event was also a triumph for espionage, deceit and thinking of the most elaborate kind

programme.title: Code-Breakers: Bletchley Park's Lost Heroes
programme.desc: Keeley Hawes narrates the story of how mathematician Bill Tutte and GPO engineer Tommy Flowers unravelled the secrets of Adolf Hitler's personal code machine - reputedly even more secure than the Enigma device. The duo's work played an important role in ending the conflict in Europe, but because of its classified nature and continued application during the Cold War, the two men were never officially recognised

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Mindfulness expert Andy Puddicombe explains how scenes from the natural world can help people to feel more positive

programme.title: Danceworks
programme.desc: Documentary that explores the work of Artistic Director Cassa Pancho as she nurtures aspiring young dancers and gains recognition for her company's collaborations with world-class choreographers. It also follows the creative process of South African dancer and choreographer Mthuthuzeli November, as he creates a dynamic new ballet inspired in equal measure by Samuel Beckett's seminal Waiting for Godot and his own African rhythms and language

programme.title: Planet Defenders
programme.desc: Environmentalist Jack has made films since he was very young and has a loyal following online. More recently he has thrown himself into climate change activism and working to increase awareness of the threats to our planet. His mission is to investigate the growing but sometimes controversial movement to rewild Britain. Jack discovers how the first legalised reintroduction of the beaver to England is having dramatic results. It is re-engineering river systems and creating rich habitats for many other species. He learns that despite the benefits of having this giant rodent around, some people think bringing it back is bad news. Jack travels to the Highlands of Scotland to witness the beauty of a 300-acre area of land that has been allowed to regenerate in its natural way for the past 40 years. It is here that he comes face to face with endangered wildlife such as red squirrels and pine martens, that have crept back from the brink of extinction to live in this wilderness. Hed also learns

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: Chelcea’s biggest passion is for hair! In this documentary, she shows us how she does her hair at home, doing her own hair, her mum’s hair, her sister’s hair, her friend’s hair and even her dad’s beard. Shemhas been doing lots of different hairstyles for everyone, inlcuding twists, braids (thick and small), plaits, tops buns, and also leaving it out as an afro, which is really good for the hair and scalp. Chelcea is an expert on the science of hair and how to look after it, and is particularity interested in natural hair remedies. Chelcea’s parents encourage their daughter’s passion and, through beautiful animation, we learn about her mum's childhood in the Democratic Republic of Congo. By taking us on her own hair journey, Chelcea’s determination to inspire others to celebrate their amazing hair shines through!

programme.title: My Life

Neisha’s dance is one of triumph and tenderness, inspiring others to find their own strength despite the obstacles that may lie in their way.

The Go Jetters need to mount a manatee rescue quickly. While Xuli attempts to stop the speedboats, Foz launches the Vroomster. programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters are going to Westman Islands, Iceland, where the world’s largest colony of Atlantic puffins gathers every year. Lars loves these tough little birds and explains how, although they spend most of their lives flying out to sea and fishing, they return to land each year to lay their eggs. When the baby birds, also called pufflings, eventually hatch, they fly out towards the ocean. But the light from nearby towns often confuses them about where to go, leading local children and adults to form 'puffin patrols' to rescue the pufflings. Lars is excited to help out but is upset when he discovers Glitch, also a fan of puffins, is already well-known on the islands for his skills as a puffin rescuer. Although Glitch offers him a place in his puffin patrol, Lars is determined to outdo Glitch with a patrol of his own - but when he recruits a clumsy Grimbot, things take a turn for the worse...

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters are in Australia, exploring the Wolfe Creek Crater, also known as Kandimalal. While Ubercorn explains how the crater was formed by a meteorite, Foz searches it for fragments of the space rock, as objects from space often contain precous metals. But soon he has competition in the form of Glitch, who races Foz to find an object that both of their metal detectors pick up - which turns out to be a rusty spoon! In the end, neither of them manage to find any pieces of meteorite, prompting Glitch to send the Grimbots to drag a huge space rock to Earth to help him! Can the Go Jetters prevent this huge disaster before it's too late?

programme.title: Maddie, the Plants and You
programme.desc: Join Maddie to discover the plants that we use in our everyday lives and how we can help to look after them and our planet. We use plants like bamboo to build with, we make clothes from plants, and we even turn plants into useful materials like rubber for making wellington boots. And wood we use for musical instruments! Along the way, we discover how latex comes from inside a rubber tree, and Maddie goes high into the rainforest canopy to show us how tall a bamboo plant grows. She also makes a bowl from a banana leaf.

programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Reau, Arlo, Oliver, Elijah, Zuri and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their babies at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Giovanna’s bag are colourful sheets of sandpaper, and all the parents and little ones have different objects and toys that feel rough. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding a safe, baby-appropriate rough object or toy inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The nursery rhyme Michael Finnegan features, as does a story involving Baby Bear and Baby Tiger, and viewers at home show how they’ve been exploring rough things too. The Baby Bear Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

programme.desc: It’s a big day in the garden as the residents prepare for the Potting Shed Amateur Dramatics Society’s annual show. Betty the Mouse is practicing her singing and Sid the Snail is getting ready for a rap, but Fred the Pill Millipede is struggling with his routine. He loves to tap dance, but it’s not easy when you have as many legs as he does. The other performers have lots of advice for Fred, but finding confidence under pressure can be a real challenge! Can he overcome his nerves in time for the big show?

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join Emily and Matthew as they discover the world around them at five months old and get stronger every day. Emily has a fun day exploring the great outdoors with her brother, sister, mum and dad. They start their day by playing with a ball, rolling it backwards and forwards between them. Emily even manages to push it away too! Meanwhile, Matthew and his family go to a local play cafe where they have fun in a colourful ball pit. Once Emily's family head out for a walk, they find a beautiful waterfall and stream and decide to have a boat race - but who will come out as the winner? When Matthew arrives at a park, he gets the chance to ride a steam train and find out how they make it go! We then follow Emily as she attends a Baby yoga class with her mum and sister. They learn how to strengthen those baby muscles before singing some songs and bouncing on a giant yoga ball. Matthew finishes his day by going down to the beach to help his siblings build a sandcastle.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear welcome little ones Dionne, Holly, Reau, Francis, Oliver and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their babies at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Nigel’s bag are a red skittle, a red fire engine toy and a red ball, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys that are red. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding a safe, baby appropriate red toy or object inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The popular baby song Little Robin Redbreast features, as does a story involving Baby Bear and Baby Tiger, and viewers at home show how they’ve been exploring red things too. The Action Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory bubbles and the Goodbye Song.
