programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Chelsea at Etihad Stadium and Liverpool v Southampton at Anfield. City and Liverpool were both out for revenge as the Citizens had lost 1-0 to Chelsea in last month's FA Cup semi-final and the Reds were defeated 1-0 by Southampton in January's reverse fixture. Plus, Leeds United v Tottenham Hotspur and Sheffield United v Crystal Palace

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, and reports from around Wales, presented by James Williams

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of classic recipes from some of the BBC's best-loved television chefs. Ainsley explores perfect pairings, ingredients that always work together beautifully. The menu includes Rachel Khoo's lavender chicken, Lorraine Pascale's squash and sweet potato lasagne, Ainsley's own duck and honey dish and an apple and cinnamon cake from Mary Berry

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Wolverhampton Wanderers v Brighton & Hove Albion (Kick-off 12.00pm). All the action from the Premier League encounter at Molineux. Both sides are likely to be disappointed with their seasons, but they put on a show in the reverse fixture at Amex Stadium in January, with the match ending in a thrilling 3-3 draw, with Wolves leading 3-2 at the end of frenetic first half, before Lewis Dunk gave the Seagulls a share of the points with a strike 20 minutes from time

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps an intrepid couple search for a home in Somerset with a budget of £900,000, complete with space for a helipad

programme.title: Inside Out (2015)
programme.desc: Inside 11-year-old Riley's head lives a team of five that controls her emotions. Led by the eternally upbeat Joy, they have always ensured she lived a happy life - but when Riley's parents move to a new city, she is plunged into depression and the emotions face a crisis that threatens their entire world. Pixar animated fantasy, with the voices of Amy Poehler and Phyllis Smith

programme.title: Snowdonia 1890
programme.desc: Heavy snow closes the quarry, but does not deter the landlord's agent from making an impromptu inspection visit. In their third week of Victorian life, the families learn they may have few rights in their harsh new world, but they do at least have each other. Narrated by Matthew Rhys

programme.title: Weatherman Walking: The Welsh Coast
programme.desc: Derek Brockway visits south Wales and discovers a walk of contrasts that includes a beach, a canal and a bustling marina. On the way he learns about Welsh tarantulas and narrowly avoids crashing a luxury boat, while wildlife expert Lizzie Daly goes on a bat hunt and party planner Onkar Singh Purewal organises a tea-party in a ghost village

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison explore Cranborne Chase, help tend to Britain's biggest modern-day maze, and meet farmers who are working together to encourage wildlife on their land. Steve Brown goes stargazing, Tom Heap reports on the possible environmental costs of a new space race launched from rural sites, and Adam Henson takes his rare breed Suffolk Punch to the maternity unit

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from the shores of Lake Windermere in the Lake District, and discovers the stories of children liberated from concentration camps who were brought to the area to start a new life. Items brought in to be valued include a Cherokee pipe, a wooden decoy duck, a First World War portrait, and a piece of footballing history

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The nation is in the grip of World Cup fever in 1966, and Fred has placed a risky accumulator bet on the outcome of the final which he starts to regret as the big match draws near. Nonnatus House awaits the arrival of four new pupil midwives, Dr Turner and Nurse Crane discover a 17-year-old boy is harbouring a desperate secret, and Cyril and Lucille's relationship continues to blossom

programme.title: The Pursuit of Love
programme.desc: Adaptation of Nancy Mitford's novel about two cousins living in 1920s Oxfordshire. Linda dreams of a life of adventure, while Fanny is more concerned with stability after being abandoned by her mother. When a glamorous local aristocrat invites a charming stranger to a ball, Linda is instantly smitten, but Fanny's disapproval threatens to tear the inseparable friends apart. With Lily James, Emily Beecham, Dominic West, Freddie Fox and Andrew Scott. Written and directed by Emily Mortimer, who co-stars as Fanny's absent mother

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by studio guests to review the day's Premier League action, including Aston Villa v Manchester United at Villa Park. United were looking to emulate their 2-1 triumph over the Villans at Old Trafford in January and keep their faint hopes of winning the title alive. Plus, Arsenal v West Bromwich Albion, West Ham United v Everton and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Brighton & Hove Albion

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Chelsea v Reading, West Ham United v Manchester City and Arsenal v Aston Villa

programme.title: Ian Wright: Home Truths
programme.desc: The footballer-turned-broadcaster investigates what effect growing up in a psychologically abusive and violent home has on children in the UK. He meets with social workers to find out how they are supporting and protecting youngsters living in at-risk households during Covid-19 as well as adults and young people who have been affected by growing up with domestic abuse. Ian also shares the story of his own formative years, shaped by a difficult relationship with his stepfather, and on the final leg of his journey visits a charity who work with people at risk of committing, or who have already committed, domestic abuse

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Angela Rippon and Julia Somerville report on gardens that have been branded dangerous after a landslide, deceptive locksmiths, and home insurance small print

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A period one-bedroom cottage near Blackwell in Worcestershire, a huge Methodist chapel in Dover in Kent and a three-bedroom end-of-terrace in Bootle on Merseyside are all sold to new, ambitious owners. Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow progress as they get underway to make their property dreams a reality

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: The cases of a council worker who spent £200,000 of public funds on horse riding and holidays, and a woman who pretended she could barely move to falsely claim benefits

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Mountain Rescue comes to the aid of a couple on a mountaineering date in the Lake District. Plus, a teenage boy jumps into a water-filled dyke to save a drowning horse

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents the show from Shrewsbury, and learns more about the world's most famous phone box. Thomas Forrester and Kate Bliss are on hand to help the teams

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.desc: Valerie gets excited by her first day of HCA training, and is then surprised when Nathan turns up at the Mill. Jimmi gets drawn into a dispute between a brother and sister

programme.title: Unbeatable

programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple to find a new home in Somerset, and visits a shopkeeper who is encouraging customers to think about their shopping habits in a bid to reduce waste

programme.desc: Sarah Moore teams up with blacksmith Kev Paxton and artisan Leigh-Anne Treadwell to transform an iron table base, a tub chair and a wooden till box

programme.desc: Arusha Irvine and David Harper hunt for antiques in Lincolnshire and Hull, coming back with a door knocker, a chocolate elephant mould, and a pair of opera glasses

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda is shocked at the sight of Zack in her home but when the intruder lets slip that Nancy wants to move out, she and Mick soon have something much more important to worry about. Now they have found Banjo, the Taylors prepare to step up their search for Bailey - but then Mitch receives a phone call about his daughter

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Donna leads the Holby nursing staff in protest - will Hanssen be moved by their cause or are things about to get much worse for them? Lucky realises that Kian lied to her about where Andrei was staying, while an accidental encounter between Josh and Hanssen threatens to spill both of their secrets

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen takes a sideways look at 'alternative Wales', featuring clips of belly dancers, hamster lovers and pylon enthusiasts

programme.title: Three Families
programme.desc: Concluding the two-part drama about the campaign to relax abortion laws in Northern Ireland. Hannah is determined to change the law and throws herself into campaigning - catapulting her into the centre of the debate. Theresa learns her trial date, but still has not told Orla that she is facing prison for what they did, and first-time older mother Rosie discovers there is no legal remedy to the complications with her pregnancy. Starring Amy James-Kelly, Sinead Keenan and Genevieve O'Reilly

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The hopefuls meet up at the Selfie Factory in London's O2 for an assignment set in the lucrative world of social media, which sees them creating fun and impactful looks on themselves to be posted on the global video-sharing platform TikTok. But as well as the make-up, they must also film and edit a video - all within the space of two hours. Back in the studio, the online theme continues as they create a look based on a face filter

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Bossin' It
programme.desc: In March 2020, university student Ellis Lloyd Jones, locked-down and bored, began turning out more and more of the TikTok videos he'd started posting five months earlier. Soon his posts began to go viral. Here, he reveals the truth behind his success. Laura Mallows reveals the meteoric and unusual rise of her vegan beauty products company Mallows Beauty. Last spring, the pandemic came as a big blow to her fledgling business, but with presence of mind Laura came up with a way out of her predicament as she became one of the first people in the UK to apply for a licence to manufacture hand-sanitizer

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman's relationship with Norma becomes strained as he grows closer to Emma. Meanwhile, Romero meets his competition for the re-elections. Thriller, starring Vera Farmiga

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Romero investigates two mysterious deaths in White Pine Bay. As Norman and Emma spend time away from the motel, buried secrets start to emerge for Caleb and Dylan, and Norma returns to school. Thriller, starring Freddie Highmore

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Chelsea at Etihad Stadium and Liverpool v Southampton at Anfield. City and Liverpool were both out for revenge as the Citizens had lost 1-0 to Chelsea in last month's FA Cup semi-final and the Reds were defeated 1-0 by Southampton in January's reverse fixture. Plus, Leeds United v Tottenham Hotspur and Sheffield United v Crystal Palace

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: The Sunday Show
programme.desc: Martin Geissler presents the main issues from the world of politics and beyond, featuring interviews, discussion and analysis on the stories that matter to Scotland

programme.title: Reflections at the Quay
programme.desc: Rev Julie Rennick of Larbert West Parish Church and Rev Dan Harper of Bridge of Allan, who both had Covid in 2020, share their Reflections. Music is by Celtic Worship

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Wolverhampton Wanderers v Brighton & Hove Albion (Kick-off 12.00pm). All the action from the Premier League encounter at Molineux. Both sides are likely to be disappointed with their seasons, but they put on a show in the reverse fixture at Amex Stadium in January, with the match ending in a thrilling 3-3 draw, with Wolves leading 3-2 at the end of frenetic first half, before Lewis Dunk gave the Seagulls a share of the points with a strike 20 minutes from time

programme.title: Sportscene Live
programme.desc: St Mirren v St Johnstone (Kick-off 2.15pm). Steven Thompson presents all the action from the second semi-final at Hampden Park. St Mirren are looking to reach the final for the first time since they lifted the trophy in 1987, but they first must overcome Premiership champions Rangers' quarter-final conquerors, who are looking to emulate their sole triumph in this competition in 2014

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates footage of emperor penguins in Atka Bay, on the coast of Antarctica, as they return from three months of feeding at sea to raise their chicks. They arrive as the sea freezes over and other animals desert the area for safer territory - but if the penguins are to nurture a future generation, they will have to work together to endure some of the harshest conditions on Earth

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which a married couple from Kent, a father and son from Luton and two flatmates from Manchester must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. Across three rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits from across the years. Whether it's a song from the swinging 60s or the number-one track in today's charts, the teams must prove their ability to recognise songs from all genres of music - from pop to country, rock to R&B and many more

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison explore Cranborne Chase, help tend to Britain's biggest modern-day maze, and meet farmers who are working together to encourage wildlife on their land. Steve Brown goes stargazing, Tom Heap reports on the possible environmental costs of a new space race launched from rural sites, and Adam Henson takes his rare breed Suffolk Punch to the maternity unit

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The nation is in the grip of World Cup fever in 1966, and Fred has placed a risky accumulator bet on the outcome of the final which he starts to regret as the big match draws near. Nonnatus House awaits the arrival of four new pupil midwives, Dr Turner and Nurse Crane discover a 17-year-old boy is harbouring a desperate secret, and Cyril and Lucille's relationship continues to blossom

programme.title: The Pursuit of Love
programme.desc: Adaptation of Nancy Mitford's novel about two cousins living in 1920s Oxfordshire. Linda dreams of a life of adventure, while Fanny is more concerned with stability after being abandoned by her mother. When a glamorous local aristocrat invites a charming stranger to a ball, Linda is instantly smitten, but Fanny's disapproval threatens to tear the inseparable friends apart. With Lily James, Emily Beecham, Dominic West, Freddie Fox and Andrew Scott. Written and directed by Emily Mortimer, who co-stars as Fanny's absent mother

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by studio guests to review the day's Premier League action, including Aston Villa v Manchester United at Villa Park. United were looking to emulate their 2-1 triumph over the Villans at Old Trafford in January and keep their faint hopes of winning the title alive. Plus, Arsenal v West Bromwich Albion, West Ham United v Everton and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Brighton & Hove Albion

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Chelsea v Reading, West Ham United v Manchester City and Arsenal v Aston Villa

programme.title: Ian Wright: Home Truths
programme.desc: The footballer-turned-broadcaster investigates what effect growing up in a psychologically abusive and violent home has on children in the UK. He meets with social workers to find out how they are supporting and protecting youngsters living in at-risk households during Covid-19 as well as adults and young people who have been affected by growing up with domestic abuse. Ian also shares the story of his own formative years, shaped by a difficult relationship with his stepfather, and on the final leg of his journey visits a charity who work with people at risk of committing, or who have already committed, domestic abuse

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Angela Rippon and Julia Somerville report on gardens that have been branded dangerous after a landslide, deceptive locksmiths, and home insurance small print

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A period one-bedroom cottage near Blackwell in Worcestershire, a huge Methodist chapel in Dover in Kent and a three-bedroom end-of-terrace in Bootle on Merseyside are all sold to new, ambitious owners. Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow progress as they get underway to make their property dreams a reality

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: The cases of a council worker who spent £200,000 of public funds on horse riding and holidays, and a woman who pretended she could barely move to falsely claim benefits

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Mountain Rescue comes to the aid of a couple on a mountaineering date in the Lake District. Plus, a teenage boy jumps into a water-filled dyke to save a drowning horse

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents the show from Shrewsbury, and learns more about the world's most famous phone box. Thomas Forrester and Kate Bliss are on hand to help the teams

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie gets excited by her first day of HCA training, and is then surprised when Nathan turns up at the Mill. Jimmi gets drawn into a dispute between a brother and sister

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple to find a new home in Somerset, and visits a shopkeeper who is encouraging customers to think about their shopping habits in a bid to reduce waste

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore teams up with blacksmith Kev Paxton and artisan Leigh-Anne Treadwell to transform an iron table base, a tub chair and a wooden till box

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine and David Harper hunt for antiques in Lincolnshire and Hull, coming back with a door knocker, a chocolate elephant mould, and a pair of opera glasses

programme.title: Inside Out (2015)
programme.desc: Inside 11-year-old Riley's head lives a team of five that controls her emotions. programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Chelsea v Reading, West Ham United v Manchester City and Arsenal v Aston Villa

programme.title: Ian Wright: Home Truths
programme.desc: The footballer-turned-broadcaster investigates what effect growing up in a psychologically abusive and violent home has on children in the UK. He meets with social workers to find out how they are supporting and protecting youngsters living in at-risk households during Covid-19 as well as adults and young people who have been affected by growing up with domestic abuse. Ian also shares the story of his own formative years, shaped by a difficult relationship with his stepfather, and on the final leg of his journey visits a charity who work with people at risk of committing, or who have already committed, domestic abuse

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Angela Rippon and Julia Somerville report on gardens that have been branded dangerous after a landslide, deceptive locksmiths, and home insurance small print

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A period one-bedroom cottage near Blackwell in Worcestershire, a huge Methodist chapel in Dover in Kent and a three-bedroom end-of-terrace in Bootle on Merseyside are all sold to new, ambitious owners. Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow progress as they get underway to make their property dreams a reality

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: The cases of a council worker who spent £200,000 of public funds on horse riding and holidays, and a woman who pretended she could barely move to falsely claim benefits

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Mountain Rescue comes to the aid of a couple on a mountaineering date in the Lake District. Plus, a teenage boy jumps into a water-filled dyke to save a drowning horse

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents the show from Shrewsbury, and learns more about the world's most famous phone box. Thomas Forrester and Kate Bliss are on hand to help the teams

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie gets excited by her first day of HCA training, and is then surprised when Nathan turns up at the Mill. Jimmi gets drawn into a dispute between a brother and sister

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple to find a new home in Somerset, and visits a shopkeeper who is encouraging customers to think about their shopping habits in a bid to reduce waste

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore teams up with blacksmith Kev Paxton and artisan Leigh-Anne Treadwell to transform an iron table base, a tub chair and a wooden till box

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine and David Harper hunt for antiques in Lincolnshire and Hull, coming back with a door knocker, a chocolate elephant mould, and a pair of opera glasses

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the studio, with the usual topical stories and celebrity chat. Presented by Alex Jones and new full-time hosts Ronan Keating and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Behind the scenes with Kent police tackling the drugs trade, and the 'county lines' set up to distribute narcotics bought in London across the county - often using children and vulnerable people as carriers. The programme examines how the illegal drugs trade has continued to flourish despite the limitations placed on movement during lockdown

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Pre-wedding jitters are at an all-time high as Callum faces the possibility that Ben will stand him up at the altar - and with Kathy thinking the same thing, she encourages Phil to speak to their son. Zack tries to help Nancy but his heroic plan does not go well, and Keegan panics when he finds Bailey's dog Banjo, alone and injured

programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: Basil goes to great lengths to prove to Sybil that a guest has smuggled a woman into his room. Instead the hotelier keeps being caught in compromising situations with a young Australian woman, and a visiting psychiatrist finds his increasingly manic behaviour both fascinating and alarming. Classic comedy first broadcast in 1979, starring John Cleese and Prunella Scales

programme.title: Three Families
programme.desc: Part one of two. Drama about the campaign to relax abortion laws in Northern Ireland, from the point of view of three families who find themselves coming into conflict with the law. A mother realises she could face a prison sentence for helping her teenage daughter to get an abortion, while a newlywed discovers her unborn baby will not survive to the end of her pregnancy - but also that she will legally still have to carry the child to term

programme.title: Road (2014)
programme.desc: For two generations the Dunlop family from Northern Ireland has dominated the dangerous world of motorcycle road racing. This documentary, narrated by Liam Neeson, tells the dramatic, tragic and inspirational story of the two sets of brothers - Joey and Robert, William and Michael - who were united by both success and, ultimately, loss

programme.title: Starstruck
programme.desc: Kate and Jessie's murder mystery party is interrupted by the arrival of Tom, in need of help. Jessie struggles to juggle her multiple love interests

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race Down Under
programme.desc: Superstar sisters Kylie and Dannii Minogue will be joining RuPaul and regular judges Michelle Visage and Rhys Nicholson for the second round of the contest, in which Australia and New Zealand's finest drag queens dress up for the Celebrity Impression Olympics

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford meets victims of a scam that's been sweeping the UK, starting with a simple text message about parcel deliveries before leading people into a terrifyingly plausible confidence trick. Plus, how some customers of one buy-and-sell website, designed to turn stash into cash, were left exposed to fraudsters

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the progress of semi-detached properties in Sneyd Green in Stoke on Trent and Low Hill in Wolverhampton

programme.title: State Opening of Parliament
programme.desc: Huw Edwards presents live coverage of the Queen's speech from Westminster, as Her Majesty outlines the government's plans for the coming year ahead of the first session of the new parliament

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Mark Stacey help two teams find items of value at Harrogate antiques fair, scouring the event in the hope that they will make a profit at auction. Amidst the highs and lows in the saleroom, presenter Anita Manning manages to find time to also embark on a quilting quest at a local museum

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie helps Nathan when he struggles to go through with meeting his birth mother. Ruhma is baffled by a young couple's approach to choosing their baby's defining characteristics

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Contestants Darren, Kerry, Mustafa and Gugu vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to win a cash prize. Quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a London couple moving to Surrey, hoping their £900,000 budget finds them a large home that is close to good schools and with easy links to the capital

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph enlists furniture restorer Katie Keat to repair a pair of wooden chairs and woodworker Carwyn Owen finds a creative solution for an unwanted pine wardrobe

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: David Harper buys an old fishing stool and Arusha Irvine splashes out on a parachuting clown as they travel through York and Durham. But only one can win at the Newcastle auction

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda is shocked at the sight of Zack in her home but when the intruder lets slip that Nancy wants to move out, she and Mick soon have something much more important to worry about. Now they have found Banjo, the Taylors prepare to step up their search for Bailey - but then Mitch receives a phone call about his daughter

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Donna leads the Holby nursing staff in protest - will Hanssen be moved by their cause or are things about to get much worse for them? Lucky realises that Kian lied to her about where Andrei was staying, while an accidental encounter between Josh and Hanssen threatens to spill both of their secrets

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste look into the case of widow Jaqueline, who ventured online to look for love, only to meet a man who targeted her life savings, stealing a six-figure sum before disappearing. However, several years on a solicitor has been in touch, claiming he has died and left Jaqueline money in his estate. Is this a genuine inheritance or just the latest stage of a sickening scam?

programme.title: Three Families
programme.desc: Concluding the two-part drama about the campaign to relax abortion laws in Northern Ireland. Hannah is determined to change the law and throws herself into campaigning - catapulting her into the centre of the debate. Theresa learns her trial date, but still has not told Orla that she is facing prison for what they did, and first-time older mother Rosie discovers there is no legal remedy to the complications with her pregnancy. Starring Amy James-Kelly, Sinead Keenan and Genevieve O'Reilly

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The hopefuls meet up at the Selfie Factory in London's O2 for an assignment set in the lucrative world of social media, which sees them creating fun and impactful looks on themselves to be posted on the global video-sharing platform TikTok. But as well as the make-up, they must also film and edit a video - all within the space of two hours. Back in the studio, the online theme continues as they create a look based on a face filter

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Bossin' It
programme.desc: In March 2020, university student Ellis Lloyd Jones, locked-down and bored, began turning out more and more of the TikTok videos he'd started posting five months earlier. Soon his posts began to go viral. Here, he reveals the truth behind his success. Laura Mallows reveals the meteoric and unusual rise of her vegan beauty products company Mallows Beauty. Last spring, the pandemic came as a big blow to her fledgling business, but with presence of mind Laura came up with a way out of her predicament as she became one of the first people in the UK to apply for a licence to manufacture hand-sanitizer

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman's relationship with Norma becomes strained as he grows closer to Emma. Meanwhile, Romero meets his competition for the re-elections. Thriller, starring Vera Farmiga

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Romero investigates two mysterious deaths in White Pine Bay. As Norman and Emma spend time away from the motel, buried secrets start to emerge for Caleb and Dylan, and Norma returns to school. Thriller, starring Freddie Highmore

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Environmental Audit select committee session on fixing fashion with evidence from Primark, Refashion, ReLondon, the Waste and Resources Action Programme and Renewal Workshop, from Wednesday 28 April.

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The House of Lords select committee on the national plan for sport and recreation looking at the barriers to sport and recreation for people from ethnic minority communities, and women and girls, with evidence from Kick It Out, Sporting Equals, Black Girls Hike, Women in Sport, the Football Association and the Muslim Sport Foundation, from Wednesday 21 April.

programme.title: Westminster Hall
programme.desc: The debate in Westminster Hall, the second Commons chamber, on the promotion of electric vehicle usage, from Wednesday 21st April.

programme.title: Northern Ireland Assembly
programme.desc: Questions to deputy First Minister Michelle O'Neill in the Northern Ireland Assembly, from Tuesday 4 May.

programme.title: Prime Properties
programme.desc: Conservative party historian Lord Lexden charts the career of Andrew Bonar Law from his birth in Canada to Downing Street, via Pembroke Lodge in Kensington

programme.title: America This Week
programme.desc: Highlights of the week in politics in the USA from C-SPAN

programme.title: Washington Journal
programme.desc: Highlights of the week from the USA

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Month by month US President Joe Biden is shifting away from Trump-era foreign policy positions. But how dramatic will the pivot be? In the middle east there are signs of a changed approach to the regions, two oil rich adversaries Saudi Arabia and Iran; more pressure on the Saudis, more engagement with Tehran. Stephen Sackur speaks to the influential boss of Saudi Arabia's Al Arabiya English news channel, Mohamed Alyahya. Have the Saudis forfeited America's unstinting support?

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers reviews the week's political news, inviting comment and analysis from Stormont and Westminster

programme.desc: The political developments of the week, with policy-makers questioned on the key issues

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Digital, Culture, Media and Sport select committee session on sport in our communities with evidence from the Sport for Development Coalition, the Sport and Recreation Alliance, UK Athletics, Swim England and the GB Wheelchair Rugby, from Tuesday 13 April

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The International Development select committee session on UK aid to Yemen with evidence from Medecins Sans Frontieres, Save the Children and the Norwegian Refugees Council followed by foreign minister James Cleverly and the HM Ambassador to Yemen, Michael Aron, from Tuesday 20 April.

programme.title: Australia with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter continues her 12,000km journey across the continent with a tour of Western Australia. She meets some of the millionaire boat-builders and surfers in the city of Perth, then moves more than 2,000km north along the coast to Broome, where she enjoys a day at the races. Finally she ventures into the Outback to find out about life with an Aboriginal family

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team go to Folkestone to create a sensory garden full of fun features for a five-year-old girl with a rare form of severe epilepsy. The quirky design incorporates a circular seating area with a vintage fireplace as a focal point, and uses special planting to bring the area to life

programme.title: Alan Titchmarsh: Spring into Summer
programme.desc: Former ballerina Darcey Bussell joins Alan to talk about bringing dance back to the nation this summer, while carpenter Wayne Perrey creates a garden bar. Plus, a couple of showstopping rare breed Gloucester cows make their TV debuts

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley takes inspiration from days beside the seaside, starting with a Caribbean squash and chickpea roti with hot sauce yoghurt, joined by former Bake Off winner Candice Brown, who puts her twist on toffee apple doughnuts. Olly Smith hunts out the perfect wines for sipping by the seashore and, as no trip to the beach would be complete without some ice cream, Ainsley has just the ticket with his homemade honeycomb sundae with butterscotch sauce

programme.title: Game of Talents
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the game show, with two contestants pairing up with Jonathan Ross and Katherine Ryan to try to win big money. They must uncover the secret talents of mystery performers ranging from a Dolly Parton impersonator to a chainsaw daredevil

programme.title: Australian Ninja Warrior
programme.desc: Rebecca Maddern, Ben Fordham and Freddie Flintoff present the Down Under version of the show in which contestants take on a formidable assault course in Melbourne, with obstacles including the Floating Steps, the Swing Surfer, the Tuning Forks, Rolling Steel, Spinball Wizard and the Double Warped Walls.

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern heads to Chester for the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges, giving them the chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the final game.

programme.title: Babe: Pig in the City (1998)
programme.desc: The pig and his farmyard pals take a trip to the big city, where their curiosity and love of adventure lands them in trouble, leaving the courageous Babe to find a way to save his chums from danger. Family comedy sequel, starring Magda Szubanski, Mickey Rooney and James Cromwell, and featuring the voice of Elizabeth Daily as Babe

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Mark Foster, Charlene White, Ade Adepitan and Kevin Eldon pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard invites Made in Chelsea's Sam Thompson, comedian Suzi Ruffell and actor and presenter Stephen Mangan to take part in a special edition of the quiz show. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: Spectre (2015)
programme.desc: James Bond goes rogue after receiving a cryptic message that sets him on the trail of a secretive criminal network. While his colleagues face a new threat closer to home, 007 embarks on a globetrotting journey in search of the elusive mastermind behind the syndicate - whom he discovers may have links to his own past and had involvement in previous events during some of Bond's most dangerous missions. Spy thriller, starring Daniel Craig, Christoph Waltz, Lea Seydoux, Ben Whishaw and Ralph Fiennes

programme.title: Trevor McDonald's Indian Train Adventure
programme.desc: Part one of two. The broadcaster explores India in luxury train the Maharajas' Express, which recaptures the heritage of the country's kings, and discovers how the country has changed since he was a foreign correspondent 30 years ago. The first part of the journey takes him to Mumbai and New Delhi, and he witnesses the contrasting nature of a growing economic superpower which still has high levels of poverty

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Billy is aghast to learn of Summer's joyriding. A terrified Kelly faces a grilling from the police. Asha gets upset at everyone doubting Corey's innocence

programme.title: The Queen and Her Cousins with Alexander Armstrong
programme.desc: As the Queen turns 95, Alexander Armstrong embarks on a royal road trip of a lifetime. He travels across the country to meet Elizabeth II's cousins, who share what it means to be part of this extraordinary family. Using the monarch's vast family tree as his road map, Alexander meets relatives from many branches of her family - from the ancient lineage of the Mountbatten dynasty, to the Queen Mother's Bowes-Lyon side - and an international princess

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: The Real 'Des': The Dennis Nilsen Story
programme.desc: Following on from the three-part drama about the serial killer Dennis Nilsen, this documentary explores the personality of the man behind the crimes - on the surface a mild-mannered civil servant who admitted to murdering up to 15 young men over a four-year period in the late 1980s and early 1980s, disposing of their bodies in the garden and down the drain. The film includes footage of an interview Nilsen gave to ITV at Albany Prison in 1992, contributions from victims' families and insights from criminologist David Wilson, who corresponded with Nilsen over several decades. Narrated by David Tennant

programme.title: Out of Their Skin
programme.desc: Part one of two. Ian Wright charts the inspiring rise of black footballers in Britain, from Viv Anderson's debut as England's first senior black player in 1978, with the aid of archive footage, original interviews and a compilation of individual stories from the past and present. Contributors include Paul Ince, Ashley Cole, Les Ferdinand, John Barnes, Raheem Sterling and Marcus Rashford

programme.desc: The hapless bumbler goes on a one-man mission to clean up the town after becoming furious with the state of the streets and all the people who drop litter. Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood films. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The popular halfwit tries to be romantic, but his idea of what that means proves to be very different to Irma's. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Ben Gwen and Grandpa Max arrive in Bismarck North Dakota for its annual celebration of bee pollination bringing them face-to-face with Queen Bee and a bold new scheme forcing Ben to conquer his new fear of bees!

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Four ninja warriors battle dark Lord Garmadon and his skeleton army

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Four ninja warriors battle dark Lord Garmadon and his skeleton army

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Weekend Best!
programme.desc: Oti Mabuse and Ricky Wilson join Martin and Roman Kemp for more celebrity chat, music and comedy, as well as games and light-hearted challenges in the which the father-and-son duo go up against their guests

programme.title: Australia with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter continues her 12,000km journey across the continent with a tour of Western Australia. She meets some of the millionaire boat-builders and surfers in the city of Perth, then moves more than 2,000km north along the coast to Broome, where she enjoys a day at the races. Finally she ventures into the Outback to find out about life with an Aboriginal family

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team go to Folkestone to create a sensory garden full of fun features for a five-year-old girl with a rare form of severe epilepsy. The quirky design incorporates a circular seating area with a vintage fireplace as a focal point, and uses special planting to bring the area to life

programme.title: Alan Titchmarsh: Spring into Summer
programme.desc: Former ballerina Darcey Bussell joins Alan to talk about bringing dance back to the nation this summer, while carpenter Wayne Perrey creates a garden bar. Plus, a couple of showstopping rare breed Gloucester cows make their TV debuts

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley takes inspiration from days beside the seaside, starting with a Caribbean squash and chickpea roti with hot sauce yoghurt, joined by former Bake Off winner Candice Brown, who puts her twist on toffee apple doughnuts. Olly Smith hunts out the perfect wines for sipping by the seashore and, as no trip to the beach would be complete without some ice cream, Ainsley has just the ticket with his homemade honeycomb sundae with butterscotch sauce

programme.title: Game of Talents
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the game show, with two contestants pairing up with Jonathan Ross and Katherine Ryan to try to win big money. They must uncover the secret talents of mystery performers ranging from a Dolly Parton impersonator to a chainsaw daredevil

programme.title: Australian Ninja Warrior
programme.desc: Rebecca Maddern, Ben Fordham and Freddie Flintoff present the Down Under version of the show in which contestants take on a formidable assault course in Melbourne, with obstacles including the Floating Steps, the Swing Surfer, the Tuning Forks, Rolling Steel, Spinball Wizard and the Double Warped Walls.

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern heads to Chester for the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges, giving them the chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the final game.

programme.title: Babe: Pig in the City (1998)
programme.desc: The pig and his farmyard pals take a trip to the big city, where their curiosity and love of adventure lands them in trouble, leaving the courageous Babe to find a way to save his chums from danger. Family comedy sequel, starring Magda Szubanski, Mickey Rooney and James Cromwell, and featuring the voice of Elizabeth Daily as Babe

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Mark Foster, Charlene White, Ade Adepitan and Kevin Eldon pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard invites Made in Chelsea's Sam Thompson, comedian Suzi Ruffell and actor and presenter Stephen Mangan to take part in a special edition of the quiz show. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: Spectre (2015)
programme.desc: James Bond goes rogue after receiving a cryptic message that sets him on the trail of a secretive criminal network. While his colleagues face a new threat closer to home, 007 embarks on a globetrotting journey in search of the elusive mastermind behind the syndicate - whom he discovers may have links to his own past and had involvement in previous events during some of Bond's most dangerous missions. Spy thriller, starring Daniel Craig, Christoph Waltz, Lea Seydoux, Ben Whishaw and Ralph Fiennes

programme.title: Trevor McDonald's Indian Train Adventure
programme.desc: Part one of two. The broadcaster explores India in luxury train the Maharajas' Express, which recaptures the heritage of the country's kings, and discovers how the country has changed since he was a foreign correspondent 30 years ago. The first part of the journey takes him to Mumbai and New Delhi, and he witnesses the contrasting nature of a growing economic superpower which still has high levels of poverty

programme.title: The Queen and Her Cousins with Alexander Armstrong
programme.desc: As the Queen turns 95, Alexander Armstrong embarks on a royal road trip of a lifetime. He travels across the country to meet Elizabeth II's cousins, who share what it means to be part of this extraordinary family. Using the monarch's vast family tree as his road map, Alexander meets relatives from many branches of her family - from the ancient lineage of the Mountbatten dynasty, to the Queen Mother's Bowes-Lyon side - and an international princess

programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: The Real 'Des': The Dennis Nilsen Story
programme.desc: Following on from the three-part drama about the serial killer Dennis Nilsen, this documentary explores the personality of the man behind the crimes - on the surface a mild-mannered civil servant who admitted to murdering up to 15 young men over a four-year period in the late 1980s and early 1980s, disposing of their bodies in the garden and down the drain. The film includes footage of an interview Nilsen gave to ITV at Albany Prison in 1992, contributions from victims' families and insights from criminologist David Wilson, who corresponded with Nilsen over several decades. Narrated by David Tennant

programme.title: Out of Their Skin
programme.desc: Part one of two. Ian Wright charts the inspiring rise of black footballers in Britain, from Viv Anderson's debut as England's first senior black player in 1978, with the aid of archive footage, original interviews and a compilation of individual stories from the past and present. Contributors include Paul Ince, Ashley Cole, Les Ferdinand, John Barnes, Raheem Sterling and Marcus Rashford

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: When their house is subjected to a compulsory purchase order, the Ropers swap urban surroundings for suburbia, where Mildred dreams of climbing the social ladder. George, on the other hand, wants to maintain as low a profile as possible. First episode of the sitcom, starring Yootha Joyce and Brian Murphy

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Omnibus. Moira and Cain set a plan in motion. Charity gives Mackenzie a warning. Mackenzie and Aaron are in trouble

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A blackmailer contaminates a jar of baby food in a local supermarket and promises it will not be the last if he does not receive payment. Wycliffe and his team spring into action, aware they are under investigation by the police disciplinary squad. Crime drama, starring Jack Shepherd and Sharon Duce

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The Cornish copper looks into a lone yachtswoman's mysterious disappearance. He learns she had been receiving threats from the owners of a local china clay works - where he later makes a grisly discovery. Jack Shepherd stars, with Con O'Neill and Michael Attwell

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Gruff detective Jack Frost's plans for a quiet weekend are disrupted by the gruesome discovery of an unidentified body floating in the river. However, soon after he has begun the investigation, he is forced to apply his deductive skills to a separate case involving an act of violence during a football match which left a player in a coma. Crime drama, starring David Jason, Larry Lamb, Bruce Alexander, Lindy Whiteford and Berwick Kaler

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: An armed robbery ends in murder and Frost wastes no time in arresting a known crook he saw at the scene during the getaway, convinced he is one of the men responsible. With this little mystery to solve, as well as a case of four burglaries in as many days at the same house, the dogged policeman's lot proves a busy one indeed. David Jason stars, with Billy Murray and James Hayes

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As Foyle prepares to be replaced at the police station and begin retirement, his attention is grabbed by a news story about a man sentenced to hang for high treason, and he decides to take on the case. But with the prisoner's execution date approaching, the detective has to work fast to find evidence that will clear him. Meanwhile, Samantha and Adam fight to save Hill House from the town planners determined to demolish it - but disaster soon strikes. Starring Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks and Max Brown

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective is approached by a former mentor from his college days, who asks Morse to ensure the safety of his wife. However, the assignment leaves him contemplating his future with the police force. Meanwhile, a murder in the world of fashion stirs up old gangland conflicts in the city. Detective drama series, starring Shaun Evans as the young Inspector Morse

programme.title: Prince Charles: Inside the Duchy of Cornwall
programme.desc: Part two of two. The documentary examines Charles's legacy for the Duchy, as senior staff prepare William for the day that he succeeds his father as the next Duke of Cornwall. The documentary also examines his well-known passion for trees and traditional rural crafts, and how these may shape the Duchy's future

programme.title: Inside the Crown: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: Documentary revealing how Queen Elizabeth II has mastered the art of being a monarch, both home and abroad, over her record-breaking 68-year reign. She is seen meeting world leaders such as Ronald Reagan, Mikhail Gorbachev and Donald Trump - although not all encounters go well, and the film also looks at the Queen's commitment to the Commonwealth, a personal project that has seen the old Empire transformed

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: When a body is dredged up from a slurry pit on a remote farm, the investigation forces owner Danny Pryor to admit he has a number of illegal Serbian workers on the payroll. A faded tattoo identifies the victim as Jack Reeves, a young man from the local travelling community, but what is his connection to the immigrants? As the case unfolds, Vera and her team must connect the dots between Jack and a wounded Serbian, and expose the tragic truth at the heart of Pryor Farm. With Mark Bonnar, Mark Womack and Jim Sturgeon

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George trades his car in for a battered caravan, and Mildred's sister and brother-in-law arrive, leading to problems in the bedroom. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy, Yootha Joyce, Norman Eshley and Sheila Fearn

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Poirot visits a garden fete where he meets the son of an old friend and his fiancee. After having tea with the couple, the sleuth suspects that they are both in danger and Hastings' new interest in photography provides him with the clue to solve a mystery involving cyanide, failing brakes and mixed-up love lives.

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A dark family secret comes to light when a female hit-and-run suspect claims the woman she deliberately ran over on a pedestrian crossing was a ghost. However, the facts of the case tell a different story. Gwen Taylor guest stars, with Jack Shepherd

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick struggles with parental responsibility alone, while coming to terms with Kate's death and juggling his police duties

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bernice tells Ashley all her troubles. Kathy talks to Graham about her relationship with Biff. Tricia and Eric plan their bid to buy the pub

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy tells Graham that she is scared of marrying Biff. Mandy clashes with Eric. Tricia sabotages Bernice's tent at the fair

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz is frustrated when Jim shows no signs of coming out of his coma. Leanne is petrified by a killer having her photo. Natalie gets tired of Samantha giving her the cold shoulder

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Leanne makes Nick sleep on the sofa. Vera comes clean about Jack's pigeons. Steve tells Liz that Jim fell after trying to hit him

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth finds himself in interesting company as English socialites and American millionaires rub shoulders on board a train bound for the French Riviera. Passengers include oil magnate Rufus Van Aldin (Elliott Gould) and his daughter Ruth (Jaime Murray) - to whom he has given a magnificent ruby known as the Heart of Fire. With members of the aristocracy crammed into a confined space with their husbands, lovers and a renowned gemstone, murder cannot be far away. With Lindsay Duncan, Roger Lloyd Pack and James D'Arcy

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective sets out for Norfolk when a country publican begs him to help solve a murder - but is disappointed when the innkeeper turns out to be a budding author and the killing fictional. However, he soon has food for thought when a real death occurs - committed by a seemingly ghostly hand. Geraldine Alexander and Ian McCulloch guest star, with David Suchet

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The discovery of a local landowner's body throws suspicion on two of his tenants, a couple who have previously been threatened with violence for refusing to move out of their cottage. Rather than stay and face the music they disappear, and Wycliffe realises if he does not get to them before the victim's family, the consequences could be deadly. Drama, starring Jack Shepherd and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: When dock worker Owen Thorne plunges from the roof of a multi-storey car park while picking up his daughter from her school prom, Vera immediately detects foul play. The team soon discovers a work dispute over the death of a young man, with everyone believing Owen was to blame, and the victim's father admits he wanted revenge - but his alibi holds. The mystery then continues when Vera spots an inconsistency in the statement Owen's wife gave to the police, suggesting she's not certain of her husband's fidelity

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Following the brutal murder of a gay man, Sam and Ronnie embark on a desperate mission to track down the victim's adoptive son, who has gone missing. The prime suspect soon emerges as the child's biological father - an alleged violent homophobe unable to stomach the idea of the youngster being brought up in a same-sex household - but was he acting alone? Guest starring Glynis Barber, with Bradley Walsh and Paul Nicholls

programme.title: Innocent
programme.desc: David Collins is unexpectedly acquitted on a technicality having served seven years of a life sentence for the murder of his wife Tara. On the steps of the court, he vows to bring to book those who lied at his original trial, to see the real killer jailed and more importantly, to regain custody of his two young children. Drama, starring Lee Ingleby

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The discovery of a woman's body in a car park presents Jack Frost with another challenge to his deductive powers. Delving into the victim's electronic organiser, he learns she was connected with a local religious community - and realises someone in the congregation is more interested in the pleasures of this world than the rewards of the next. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander and John Lyons

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George pushes Mildred to choose between him and the dog when he begins to resent the affection lavished upon her beloved pooch

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective sets out for Norfolk when a country publican begs him to help solve a murder - but is disappointed when the innkeeper turns out to be a budding author and the killing fictional. However, he soon has food for thought when a real death occurs - committed by a seemingly ghostly hand. Geraldine Alexander and Ian McCulloch guest star, with David Suchet

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The discovery of a local landowner's body throws suspicion on two of his tenants, a couple who have previously been threatened with violence for refusing to move out of their cottage. Rather than stay and face the music they disappear, and Wycliffe realises if he does not get to them before the victim's family, the consequences could be deadly. Drama, starring Jack Shepherd and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Ruby is attempting to win Blaketons heart, and Nick's colleagues are enjoying teasing him over his mum's potential romance with the sergeant. An illiterate man, Eddie, is outraged when a cottage to rent was promised to him by his aunt, but instead ends up being sold. His girlfriend calls off their wedding because they don't have a house! There is also an investigation of a peeping Tom in the village. Auntie Eileen comes to visit, and Ruby sees her chance to return to London

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff is over the moon that Kathy has accepted his proposal. Mandy awaits her trial at the magistrates court. Graham tells Kathy he has decided to leave the village

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy tries to change Graham's mind about leaving. Stella invites Bernice and Gavin for tea at Home Farm. Butch sees Emily's dad hitting her

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Zoe jumps into the canal with Morgan when the police attempt to reach out to her. Gary and Nick dive in to try to save them. Liz is delighted when Jim comes out of his coma

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz remains at Jim's side as he attempts to communicate with her. Maxine fears that Fiona will sack her. Ashley feels Zoe would be better off without him

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. Poirot is called in by his solicitor friend Gilbert Entwhistle, to ascertain whether the late Mr Abernethie met with foul play - and also finds time to investigate the murder of a beneficiary under the new terms of the will. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Robert Bathurst, Anna Calder-Marshall and Geraldine James

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Beautiful Russian aristocrat Countess Vera Rossakoff, the chief suspect in a series of jewel thefts, seemingly captivates Poirot with her charm and intellect, meaning Hastings and Miss Lemon have to do most of the actual detective work and ensure she is brought to justice. Drama, guest starring Kika Markham

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: When an unpopular pub landlord is beaten to death, a police informant casts suspicion on his ex-wife and her new husband. It seems like an open-and-shut case - but Lane badly mishandles the entire investigation, forcing Wycliffe and Kersey to intervene. Jack Shepherd stars as the Cornish copper

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When Dr Alan Delaney is killed by a hit-and-run driver while in charge of his partner's new car, Barnaby and Jones discover the dead man had once been accused by Dr James Kirkwood of stealing a large sum of money from their Midsomer Market practice. Further investigations to uncover the whereabouts of the missing money lead the detectives to believe that Kirkwood may have been the real target. Guest starring Stephanie Cole (Doc Martin), with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Return of the detective drama starring Bradley Walsh. Ronnie and new partner Joe lead the investigation into the death of jeweller Harry Bernstein, who has been found with his hands and teeth missing. His wife, her lover and a former business associate are all possible suspects, but the case takes a surprising turn when the victim's sister receives a gruesome delivery. She's on the jury for the trial of a drug dealer and murderer who Ronnie has been trying to nail for years, and the threat to her points to jury tampering, so Jake and Kate have to take a dramatic approach to win the case. The problem is, they're up against a formidable defence barrister. Co-starring Ben Bailey Smith, Dominic Rowan and Georgia Taylor, with guests Helen Baxendale and Christopher Fulford

programme.title: Innocent
programme.desc: As DI Hudson's new investigation unearths serious flaws in the original murder investigation, those who were closest to Tara are forced to explain away credible motives for wanting to kill her. Relationships are strained to breaking and damaging accusations fly when David confronts his brother and sister in law over access to his children

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A student is attacked on campus, and Jack takes time off from questioning two robbery suspects to catch the thug responsible before he can strike again. The trail leads him to Boxborough - where a confrontation with a belligerent solicitor provides the clue he needs to crack the case. Starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons, Charlotte Bellamy and Tazmin Malleson

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: When their house is subjected to a compulsory purchase order, the Ropers swap urban surroundings for suburbia, where Mildred dreams of climbing the social ladder. George, on the other hand, wants to maintain as low a profile as possible. First episode of the sitcom, starring Yootha Joyce and Brian Murphy

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Omnibus. Moira and Cain set a plan in motion. Charity gives Mackenzie a warning. Mackenzie and Aaron are in trouble

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A blackmailer contaminates a jar of baby food in a local supermarket and promises it will not be the last if he does not receive payment. Wycliffe and his team spring into action, aware they are under investigation by the police disciplinary squad. Crime drama, starring Jack Shepherd and Sharon Duce

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The Cornish copper looks into a lone yachtswoman's mysterious disappearance. He learns she had been receiving threats from the owners of a local china clay works - where he later makes a grisly discovery. Jack Shepherd stars, with Con O'Neill and Michael Attwell

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Gruff detective Jack Frost's plans for a quiet weekend are disrupted by the gruesome discovery of an unidentified body floating in the river. However, soon after he has begun the investigation, he is forced to apply his deductive skills to a separate case involving an act of violence during a football match which left a player in a coma. Crime drama, starring David Jason, Larry Lamb, Bruce Alexander, Lindy Whiteford and Berwick Kaler

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: An armed robbery ends in murder and Frost wastes no time in arresting a known crook he saw at the scene during the getaway, convinced he is one of the men responsible. With this little mystery to solve, as well as a case of four burglaries in as many days at the same house, the dogged policeman's lot proves a busy one indeed. David Jason stars, with Billy Murray and James Hayes

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As Foyle prepares to be replaced at the police station and begin retirement, his attention is grabbed by a news story about a man sentenced to hang for high treason, and he decides to take on the case. But with the prisoner's execution date approaching, the detective has to work fast to find evidence that will clear him. Meanwhile, Samantha and Adam fight to save Hill House from the town planners determined to demolish it - but disaster soon strikes. Starring Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks and Max Brown

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective is approached by a former mentor from his college days, who asks Morse to ensure the safety of his wife. However, the assignment leaves him contemplating his future with the police force. Meanwhile, a murder in the world of fashion stirs up old gangland conflicts in the city. Detective drama series, starring Shaun Evans as the young Inspector Morse

programme.title: Prince Charles: Inside the Duchy of Cornwall

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A huge scandal threatens Bohemia, as Holmes is outwitted by a bewitching woman's blackmail who owns a compromising photograph of a European royal. programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur spots a potential darts champion and enters him in a contest, where he duly gets nobbled. Comedy drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Big Fish Off

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: TV presenter Adrian Chiles, former Hollyoaks star Gemma Merna (Carmel Valentine), Holby City's Rosie Marcel (Jac Naylor) and broadcaster Jon Snow compete to win thousands of pounds for charity by taking on one of the show's resident trivia experts in a test of their general knowledge. However, they must work as a team to build their prize pot and stay one step ahead of the Chaser. Bradley Walsh hosts

programme.title: Monaco Historique Grand Prix Highlights
programme.desc: A celebration of the Monaco Grand Prix, as machinery from different eras, graced by the likes of Stirling Moss, Graham Hill, Jackie Stewart, Niki Lauda, Alain Prost and Ayrton Senna, is put through its paces on the famous Monaco streets

programme.title: Rambo: First Blood Part II (1985)
programme.desc: Incarcerated Special Forces veteran John Rambo is offered the chance for freedom by former commander Colonel Trautman if he takes up the offer to return to the jungles of Vietnam on a life-or-death mission to rescue American PoWs. During the course of the operation, he develops strong feelings for his guide, a female freedom fighter - but tragedy strikes, leaving him more determined than ever to exact a bloody and explosive revenge. Action adventure sequel, starring Sylvester Stallone, Richard Crenna, Julia Nickson-Soul, Steven Berkoff, George Kee Cheung, Andy Wood and Martin Kove

What does the fate of these creatures say about the health of Europe's second longest river?
